1	# Changes
2	
3	- Windowed-UI saga step 4 (carousel layout): third layout mode with a focused primary at ~80% of viewport + prev/next peeks (~9%) on either side, plus `[◀]` / `[▶]` cycle buttons in the top bar. `computeLayout` gains a carousel branch that filters out manually-minimized agents (they stay in the bottom dock, deliverable 5) and builds a name-ordered ring: focus defaults to `selectedId` → coordinator → first, prev/next wrap around the ends, and n≤2 collapses to just the primary or primary+one peek. `setLayoutMode` now accepts `'carousel'` (leaves stack mode when coming from stack, clears any lingering maximized state so carousel/stack math isn't short-circuited). New `carouselCycle(delta)` moves `selectedId` through the eligible ring; `.carousel-btn[data-carousel]` buttons wire prev/next. `windowSelect` re-runs the layout when carousel is active so clicking a header snaps that window to the primary slot. Compact-tile `swap-to-focus` handler also updates `selectedId` so clicking a peek (rendered compact at 9% width) promotes it. Dock-cell clicks in carousel restore + focus. `#carousel-nav` toggles via `updateCarouselNav`. Live browser smoke (3-agent demo): Tiled → Carousel lays out coord primary (1126px of 1400), worker-rpg left peek (125px), worker-hlasm right peek (125px); `◀/▶` cycle shuffles name-order ring; peek click jumps to center; manually minimized worker-hlasm goes to dock and is skipped by cycle; dock-click restores + focuses; Tiled toggle back restores 3-tile grid; persistence round-trips. cargo test + clippy clean.

# Changes

- Windowed-UI saga step 4 (carousel layout): third layout mode with a focused primary at ~80% of viewport + prev/next peeks (~9%) on either side, plus `[◀]` / `[▶]` cycle buttons in the top bar. `computeLayout` gains a carousel branch that filters out manually-minimized agents (they stay in the bottom dock, deliverable 5) and builds a name-ordered ring: focus defaults to `selectedId` → coordinator → first, prev/next wrap around the ends, and n≤2 collapses to just the primary or primary+one peek. `setLayoutMode` now accepts `'carousel'` (leaves stack mode when coming from stack, clears any lingering maximized state so carousel/stack math isn't short-circuited). New `carouselCycle(delta)` moves `selectedId` through the eligible ring; `.carousel-btn[data-carousel]` buttons wire prev/next. `windowSelect` re-runs the layout when carousel is active so clicking a header snaps that window to the primary slot. Compact-tile `swap-to-focus` handler also updates `selectedId` so clicking a peek (rendered compact at 9% width) promotes it. Dock-cell clicks in carousel restore + focus. `#carousel-nav` toggles via `updateCarouselNav`. Live browser smoke (3-agent demo): Tiled → Carousel lays out coord primary (1126px of 1400), worker-rpg left peek (125px), worker-hlasm right peek (125px); `◀/▶` cycle shuffles name-order ring; peek click jumps to center; manually minimized worker-hlasm goes to dock and is skipped by cycle; dock-click restores + focuses; Tiled toggle back restores 3-tile grid; persistence round-trips. cargo test + clippy clean.

# Changes

input.new_string:
# Changes

- Windowed-UI saga step 5 (Option-C keyboard): bare-key window-manager bindings that fire only when focus isn't in an input/textarea/xterm. Replaced the legacy scale-UI keyboard handler (1..9 hottest-unpinned, 0 coord, f focus-size, p pin-focused, `/`, Esc clear-focus) with the selected-window model: `m` minimize selected, `M` (Shift-m) maximize/restore toggle, `p` pin/unpin, `←/→` cycle focus prev/next by sort order (excluding minimized), `1..9` jump to Nth in sort order, `/` focuses the filter input, `Esc` restores any maximized window on first press then deselects on second. Extra `isXtermFocused()` guard checks `document.activeElement.closest('.xterm')` so xterm's own textarea doesn't leak bindings through. Helpers: `keyboardCycleItems`, `keyboardJump`, `keyboardCycle`. Top-bar `#kbd-hint` strip shows the bindings as muted text next to the carousel nav. Per-panel `.pty-focus-badge` ("typing to PTY", amber pill near Send) toggles via a `focusin`/`focusout` document listener that tracks whether the active element is inside an `.xterm`, adding `.pty-focused` to the owning `.agent-panel`. Live browser smoke (3-agent demo): click coord header → `m` minimizes it (moves to dock); select hlasm → `M` maximizes to 1113px (~80%); `Esc` restores, second `Esc` deselects; `2` jumps to the 2nd cycle item; `9` is a no-op with only 2 items; `←`/`→` wrap; `p` pins at the current rect; focusing the xterm textarea then pressing `m` is correctly ignored (selection unchanged, no minimize). cargo test + clippy clean.
- Windowed-UI saga step 4 (carousel layout):

- Windowed-UI saga step 5 (Option-C keyboard): bare-key window-manager bindings that fire only when focus isn't in an input/textarea/xterm. lines: +- Windowed-UI saga step 5 (Option-C keyboard): bare-key window-manager bindings that fire only when focus isn't in an input/textarea/xterm. Replaced the legacy scale-UI keyboard handler (1..9 hottest-unpinned, 0 coord, f focus-size, p pin-focused, `/`, Esc clear-focus) with the selected-window model: `m` minimize selected, `M` (Shift-m) maximize/restore toggle, `p` pin/unpin, `←/→` cycle focus prev/next by sort order (excluding minimized), `1..9` jump to Nth in sort order, `/` focuses the filter input, `Esc` restores any maximized window on first press then deselects on second. Extra `isXtermFocused()` guard checks `document.activeElement.closest('.xterm')` so xterm's own textarea doesn't leak bindings through. Helpers: `keyboardCycleItems`, `keyboardJump`, `keyboardCycle`. Top-bar `#kbd-hint` strip shows the bindings as muted text next to the carousel nav. Per-panel `.pty-focus-badge` ("typing to PTY", amber pill near Send) toggles via a `focusin`/`focusout` document listener that tracks whether the active element is inside an `.xterm`, adding `.pty-focused` to the owning `.agent-panel`. Live browser smoke (3-agent demo): click coord header → `m` minimizes it (moves to dock); select hlasm → `M` maximizes to 1113px (~80%); `Esc` restores, second `Esc` deselects; `2` jumps to the 2nd cycle item; `9` is a no-op with only 2 items; `←`/`→` wrap; `p` pins at the current rect; focusing the xterm textarea then pressing `m` is correctly ignored (selection unchanged, no minimize). cargo test + clippy clean.

- Windowed-UI saga step 4 (carousel layout): third layout mode with a focused primary at ~80% of viewport + prev/next peeks (~9%) on either side, plus `[◀]` / `[▶]` cycle buttons in the top bar. `computeLayout` gains a carousel branch that filters out manually-minimized agents (they stay in the bottom dock, deliverable 5) and builds a name-ordered ring: focus defaults to `selectedId` → coordinator → first, prev/next wrap around the ends, and n≤2 collapses to just the primary or primary+one peek. `setLayoutMode` now accepts `'carousel'` (leaves stack mode when coming from stack, clears any lingering maximized state so carousel/stack math isn't short-circuited). New `carouselCycle(delta)` moves `selectedId` through the eligible ring; `.carousel-btn[data-carousel]` buttons wire prev/next. `windowSelect` re-runs the layout when carousel is active so clicking a header snaps that window to the primary slot. Compact-tile `swap-to-focus` handler also updates `selectedId` so clicking a peek (rendered compact at 9% width) promotes it. Dock-cell clicks in carousel restore + focus. `#carousel-nav` toggles via `updateCarouselNav`. Live browser smoke (3-agent demo): Tiled → Carousel lays out coord primary (1126px of 1400), worker-rpg left peek (125px), worker-hlasm right peek (125px); `◀/▶` cycle shuffles name-order ring; peek click jumps to center; manually minimized worker-hlasm goes to dock and is skipped by cycle; dock-click restores + focuses; Tiled toggle back restores 3-tile grid; persistence round-trips. cargo test + clippy clean.

- Windowed-UI saga step 3 (stack layout): new `Tiled` / `Stack` toggle in the top bar (`.layout-selector`). Switching to Stack reconciles per-window state via `enterStackMode` — one primary (state: `normal`, derived from `selectedId` → coordinator role → first by sort) and everyone else dock-minimized; `computeLayout` short-circuits on `layoutMode === 'stack'` to place the primary centered at ~80% × 90% of viewport and emit the rest as implicit dock entries. Clicking a dock cell in Stack mode routes to new `stackPromote` (swap which window is normal vs minimized + select the clicked); dock-cell clicks in Tiled still restore as before. Switching back to Tiled runs `leaveStackMode` and restores every non-pinned window to `normal`. New agents added while Stack is active auto-join minimized via `addAgentPanel`. Startup re-runs `enterStackMode` if the persisted `layoutMode` is `stack` so refreshes render consistently. Helpers: `pickStackPrimary`, `setLayoutMode`, `updateLayoutSelector`, `setupLayoutSelector`. Live browser smoke (3-agent demo): Tiled → Stack yields 1 normal / 2 minimized with coord primary; dock-click swaps primary to worker-hlasm; Tiled restores 3 normals; persistence round-trips via `atn-scale-ui-v1`. cargo test + clippy clean.

- Windowed-UI saga step 2 (window chrome): each panel header now carries six icon buttons (`_` minimize / `□` maximize-restore toggle / `📌` pin / `▸` config / `↻` reconnect / `✕` delete). `layoutState.windows[id] = { state: 'normal'|'minimized'|'maximized', pinned }` tracks per-window state (plus `layoutState.selectedId` for click-select). Clicking a header outlines that window in accent green (the "selected" window — where step-5 bare-key shortcuts will act). Clicking a chrome icon fires its action without selecting. Minimized windows move off-screen; a new `#window-dock` strip along the bottom of the dashboard renders a 180×48 cell per minimized agent (role + name + state badge + last-line). Clicking a dock cell restores that agent. Maximize lifts one agent to ~80% of viewport centered; all others are implicitly dock-minimized for the duration (their stored state stays unchanged). `windowSelect` / `windowMinimize` / `windowMaximizeToggle` / `windowRestore` plus `reconnectAgent` + `confirmDelete` handlers wire the chrome to the state map. Dock rendering lives in `renderWindowDock`; `escapeHTML` helper used throughout. 60 workspace tests still green; clippy clean.

input.command:
git add CHANGES.md crates/atn-server/static/index.html && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(ui): Option-C keyboard — m/M/p/←→/1..9/Esc on selected window

Step 5 of the windowed-UI saga. Replaces the scale-UI keyboard
handler (hottest-N, focus-size toggle) with a window-manager model
keyed off selectedId:

  m      minimize selected
  M      maximize / restore toggle
  p      pin / unpin (lock in place)
  ←/→    cycle focus by sort order (skips minimized)
  1..9   jump to Nth in sort order
  /      focus filter input
  Esc    restore any maximized first, else deselect

Guarded by isTypingTarget + an extra isXtermFocused check
(document.activeElement.closest('.xterm')) so xterm's hidden
textarea doesn't leak bindings through. New #kbd-hint strip in
the top bar shows the bindings as muted text. Per-panel
.pty-focus-badge ("typing to PTY") appears near Send when the
panel's xterm has focus, via a focusin/focusout listener that
adds .pty-focused to the owning .agent-panel.

Verified each binding live against the 3-agent demo.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

agentrail complete \
  --summary "Replaced legacy keyboard handler with Option-C window-manager bindings: m minimize, M maximize/restore, p pin, ←/→ cycle, 1..9 jump, Esc restore-then-deselect. Guarded by isTypingTarget + isXtermFocused. New #kbd-hint strip + per-panel .pty-focus-badge. All bindings verified live." \
  --reward 1 \
  --actions "Added .kbd-hint + .pty-focus-badge CSS and corresponding HTML (hint in top bar, badge in each .agent-controls). New helpers isXtermFocused/keyboardCycleItems/keyboardJump/keyboardCycle. Rewrote document keydown listener: m/M/p act on layoutState.selectedId, ArrowLeft/Right call keyboardCycle, digit keys call keyboardJump, / focuses filter input, Escape calls windowRestore(hasMaximized()) then windowSelect(null). Added focusin/focusout document listeners that toggle .pty-focused on owning .agent-panel when activeElement is inside .xterm. Verified: click-to-select lands on coord, m minimizes to dock, M on hlasm maximizes to 1113px, Esc restores then deselects, digit jump + ArrowLeft/Right cycle skip minimized, p pins selected, xterm textarea focus correctly blocks bindings."

content.text: Step 5 (keyboard-option-C) complete. New bindings wired on top of the `selectedId` model: `m` minimize, `M` maximize/restore, `p` pin, `←/→` cycle, `1..9` jump, `Esc` restore-then-deselect. Guarded by `isTypingTarget` + `isXtermFocused`. Top-bar hint + per-panel "typing to PTY" badge round out the UX. Server still at http://localhost:7500/ — hard-refresh to try the bindings. Ready for step 6 (persistence-docs-demo) in the next session.

content.content:
=== PLAN ===
ATN — Windowed-UI Redesign

The scale-UI treemap worked in theory but drifts in practice: heat
decay shifts tile sizes, squarify flips ordering on ties, scale-snap
picks oscillate, and users get stuck in states they can't recover from.
Replace it with a conventional desktop-windowing model.

## Design (agreed with user)

Window states (per agent): `normal` | `minimized` | `maximized`.

Layout modes (mutually exclusive):
  - **Tiled** — grid of equal tiles; coord top-left, workers by
    sort order
  - **Stack** — one primary window ~80%; everything else minimized
    to a strip
  - **Carousel** — one focused window ~80% + peek of prev/next;
    ◀/▶ cycles

Sort: `name` (default, coord-first) | `recent` (most-recent-output
first; heat endpoint keeps powering this, no tile-area impact).

Per-window chrome: always-visible header with [_] minimize,
[□]/[❐] maximize/restore toggle, [📌] pin toggle, [✕] delete,
[▸] config. Plus the existing Send/Ctrl-C/Restart/Reconnect/Stop
controls below the xterm.

Top-bar controls: layout selector, sort selector, [◀] [▶] focus
cycler. No Refresh button — nothing auto-updates; any UI change is
an explicit user action or agent add/remove.

Keyboard (option C — click-to-select, no mode):
  - Clicking a window's **header bar** selects it (accent outline).
  - Bare keys `m` / `M` / `p` / `←/→` / `1..9` / `Esc` only act
    when no input/textarea/xterm currently has focus.
  - Clicking **inside** an xterm puts focus there; all keys route
    to the PTY. Clicking empty dashboard or pressing `Esc` frees.
  - The **Send** field below each xterm remains the primary text
    path to agents, so most users never click into xterm directly.

Defaults: layout = **Stack**, sort = **name**. Coord is "the most
prominent window" in every layout — it's top-left in Tiled,
primary in Stack, center in Carousel's initial position.

## Steps

1. **tiled-foundation** — strip the treemap/squarify/heat-sizing
   machinery; replace with a simple tiled-grid placement function
   (coord top-left, workers in name order, equal-sized). Keep the
   existing per-panel controls intact for now. localStorage carries
   `layoutMode: 'tiled'` (only value supported this step) and
   `sortMode: 'name'`.

2. **window-chrome** — new titlebar per window with minimize /
   maximize-restore / pin / delete / config icons. Click header to
   select the window (accent outline); click xterm body to focus the
   PTY. Minimize collapses to a bottom strip cell (live state badge
   + last-line preview). Maximize fills ~80% of viewport, pushing
   others to minimized. Restore brings them back.

3. **stack-layout** — add the Stack mode: one primary window
   at ~80% viewport + strip of minimized windows at bottom. Primary
   = focused/selected, else coord. Switching from tiled → stack
   auto-minimizes non-primary.

4. **carousel-layout** — add Carousel: focused window ~80% center,
   prev/next peek at edges. ◀/▶ cycle buttons in top bar.

5. **keyboard-option-C** — bare-key shortcuts act on the currently
   selected window (or the focused in carousel). `m` minimize, `M`
   maximize toggle, `p` pin toggle, `←/→` focus prev/next, `1..9`
   jump, `Esc` deselect / restore-all. Guard: isTypingTarget skips
   when xterm/input/textarea has focus. Keyboard hint line in the
   top bar.

6. **persistence-docs-demo** — localStorage persists layoutMode,
   sortMode, pinned set, per-window state. docs/demos-scripts.md
   gets a new Demo 9 "windowed UI" and demo 6 (treemap) gets an
   "archived" note. docs/windowed-ui.md walkthrough. Old
   scale-ui.md stays but is linked as legacy.

=== STEPS ===
  [x] 001-tiled-foundation [production]: ## Step 1: Tiled Foundation

Replace the heat-driven treemap layout with a si...
  [x] 002-window-chrome [production]: ## Step 2: Window Chrome + Click-to-Select

Add always-visible window control...
  [x] 003-stack-layout [production]: ## Step 3: Stack Layout

Add the Stack layout mode: one primary window at ~80...
  [x] 004-carousel-layout [production]: ## Step 4: Carousel Layout

Add the Carousel layout: focused window ~80% cent...
  [x] 005-keyboard-option-c [production]: ## Step 5: Keyboard — Option C

Bare-key shortcuts that only fire when no x...
  [ ] 006-windowed-ui-docs [production]: ## Step 6: Persistence, Docs, Demo

Final polish: wire localStorage for the n... <-- YOU ARE HERE

=== CURRENT STEP: 006-windowed-ui-docs [production] ===
Status: pending
Description: ## Step 6: Persistence, Docs, Demo

Final polish: wire localStorage for the n...

=== PROMPT ===
## Step 6: Persistence, Docs, Demo

Final polish: wire localStorage for the new state shape, update
docs, add a demo, mark old scale-UI docs as legacy.

### Deliverables

1. localStorage key `atn-window-ui-v1` persists:
   - `layoutMode` (tiled / stack / carousel)
   - `sortMode` (name / recent)
   - `selectedId`
   - Per-agent `windows[id] = { state, pinned }` for states that
     diverge from defaults
2. `docs/windowed-ui.md` — walkthrough covering: layouts, chrome
   controls, click-to-select, keyboard Option C, sort modes,
   pin/minimize/maximize lifecycle, saved layouts persistence.
3. `docs/demos-scripts.md` gains Demo 9 "Windowed UI" and Demo 6
   (treemap scale-UI) is marked `(legacy)` with a pointer at the
   new doc.
4. `docs/scale-ui.md` gains a banner at the top linking to
   `windowed-ui.md` and noting it's the preferred model now.
5. `docs/usage.md` Dashboard section rewritten around windows.
6. `docs/status.md` adds W1..W6 rows for this saga.
7. `demos/windowed-ui/setup.sh` — identical topology to
   `demos/claude-opencode/setup.sh` (1 coord + 3 workers) but
   written to showcase the windowed model (post-message hinting
   the user to try minimizing, switching layouts, keyboard
   shortcuts).

### Acceptance

- Setting layoutMode/sortMode/pins survives a browser hard-refresh.
- Docs are followable end-to-end.
- cargo doc --workspace --no-deps warning-free.

=== WHEN DONE ===
Run: agentrail begin    (if not yet started)
Run: agentrail complete --summary "what you did" --next-slug <slug> --next-prompt "next instructions"
  Or: agentrail complete --summary "what you did" --done   (if this is the last step)

   `windowed-ui.md` and noting it's the preferred model now.
5. `docs/usage.md` Dashboard section rewritten around windows.
6. `docs/status.md` adds W1..W6 rows for this saga.
7. `demos/windowed-ui/setup.sh` — identical topology to
   `demos/claude-opencode/setup.sh` (1 coord + 3 workers) but
   written to showcase the windowed model (post-message hinting
   the user to try minimizing, switching layouts, keyboard
   shortcuts).

### Acceptance

- Setting layoutMode/sortMode/pins survives a browser hard-refresh.
- Docs are followable end-to-end.
- cargo doc --workspace --no-deps warning-free.

=== WHEN DONE ===
Run: agentrail begin    (if not yet started)
Run: agentrail complete --summary "what you did" --next-slug <slug> --next-prompt "next instructions"
  Or: agentrail complete --summary "what you did" --done   (if this is the last step)

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# demos/claude-opencode/setup.sh
3	#
4	# Stand up the classic 4-agent local demo:
5	#   1 coordinator  (claude)    + 3 workers (opencode)
6	# Every agent is transport=local, working_dir=`.`, so it doesn't need a
7	# remote host. Useful for demoing the treemap + focus + pin flow when
8	# you don't have queenbee handy.
9	#
10	# Usage
11	#   # Default: fake shims via tools/ — no real LLM CLIs needed.
12	#   ./demos/claude-opencode/setup.sh
13	#
14	#   # Real CLIs from PATH (needs claude + opencode installed).
15	#   ATN_DEMO_REAL=1 ./demos/claude-opencode/setup.sh
16	#
17	#   # Reuse an already-running server:
18	#   ATN_DEMO_SKIP_BOOT=1 ATN_DEMO_URL=http://localhost:7500 \
19	#     ./demos/claude-opencode/setup.sh
20	#
21	# Environment
22	#   ATN_DEMO_REAL       — `1` uses real CLIs on PATH; default uses fake shims.
23	#   ATN_DEMO_SKIP_BOOT  — `1` skips atn-server launch and posts against
24	#                         $ATN_DEMO_URL (default http://localhost:7500).
25	#   ATN_DEMO_URL        — Base URL of the server.
26	
27	set -euo pipefail
28	
29	script_dir="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"
30	repo_root="$(cd "$script_dir/../.." && pwd)"
31	
32	url="${ATN_DEMO_URL:-http://localhost:7500}"
33	server_pid=""
34	
35	if [ "${ATN_DEMO_REAL:-0}" = "1" ]; then
36	    coordinator_agent="claude"
37	    worker_agent="opencode"
38	    echo "demo: ATN_DEMO_REAL=1 — using real claude / opencode on PATH"
39	else
40	    export PATH="$repo_root/tools:$PATH"
41	    coordinator_agent="fake-claude"
42	    worker_agent="fake-opencode-glm5"
43	    echo "demo: using fake shims (fake-claude, fake-opencode-glm5); set ATN_DEMO_REAL=1 for real"
44	fi
45	
46	wait_for_ready() {
47	    for _ in $(seq 1 50); do
48	        if curl -s --max-time 1 "$url/api/agents" > /dev/null; then
49	            return 0
50	        fi
51	        sleep 0.2
52	    done
53	    echo "error: $url/api/agents never responded" >&2
54	    return 1
55	}
56	
57	if [ "${ATN_DEMO_SKIP_BOOT:-0}" != "1" ]; then
58	    if curl -s --max-time 1 "$url/api/agents" > /dev/null; then
59	        echo "demo: $url already reachable — reusing running server"
60	    else
61	        echo "demo: booting atn-server (logs at /tmp/atn-claude-opencode.log)"
62	        (
63	            cd "$repo_root"
64	            ./target/debug/atn-server agents.toml
65	        ) > /tmp/atn-claude-opencode.log 2>&1 &
66	        server_pid=$!
67	        trap 'if [ -n "$server_pid" ]; then echo "demo: stopping server pid=$server_pid"; kill "$server_pid" 2>/dev/null || true; fi' EXIT
68	    fi
69	fi
70	
71	wait_for_ready
72	echo "demo: server ready at $url"
73	
74	post_agent() {
75	    local name="$1" role="$2" agent_bin="$3"
76	    local body
77	    body=$(cat <<EOF
78	{
79	    "name": "$name",
80	    "role": "$role",
81	    "transport": "local",
82	    "working_dir": ".",
83	    "project": "claude-opencode",
84	    "agent": "$agent_bin"
85	}
86	EOF
87	    )
88	    local code
89	    code=$(curl -sS -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' \
90	        -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
91	        --data-binary "$body" \
92	        "$url/api/agents")
93	    printf '  %-16s %s\n' "$name" "$code"
94	}
95	
96	echo "demo: spawning 4 agents..."
97	post_agent coord       coordinator "$coordinator_agent"
98	post_agent worker-1    worker      "$worker_agent"
99	post_agent worker-2    worker      "$worker_agent"
100	post_agent worker-3    worker      "$worker_agent"
101	
102	echo
103	echo "demo: 4 agents up. Open $url to watch them."
104	echo
105	echo "Try the per-agent Config button to switch a worker from local to mosh,"
106	echo "change its agent (e.g. fake-codex → fake-claude), or point it at a"
107	echo "different dir. (See docs/demos-scripts.md Demo 6 for the full"
108	echo "treemap / pin / keyboard walkthrough.)"
109	echo
110	echo "To tear down just these agents (leaves the server running):"
111	echo "  for id in coord worker-1 worker-2 worker-3; do"
112	echo "    curl -X DELETE $url/api/agents/\$id"
113	echo "  done"
114	
115	if [ -n "$server_pid" ]; then
116	    echo "demo: Ctrl-C to stop the server this script launched."
117	    wait "$server_pid"
118	fi
119

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# demos/claude-opencode/setup.sh
#
# Stand up the classic 4-agent local demo:
#   1 coordinator  (claude)    + 3 workers (opencode)
# Every agent is transport=local, working_dir=`.`, so it doesn't need a
# remote host. Useful for demoing the treemap + focus + pin flow when
# you don't have queenbee handy.
#
# Usage
#   # Default: fake shims via tools/ — no real LLM CLIs needed.
#   ./demos/claude-opencode/setup.sh
#
#   # Real CLIs from PATH (needs claude + opencode installed).
#   ATN_DEMO_REAL=1 ./demos/claude-opencode/setup.sh
#
#   # Reuse an already-running server:
#   ATN_DEMO_SKIP_BOOT=1 ATN_DEMO_URL=http://localhost:7500 \
#     ./demos/claude-opencode/setup.sh
#
# Environment
#   ATN_DEMO_REAL       — `1` uses real CLIs on PATH; default uses fake shims.
#   ATN_DEMO_SKIP_BOOT  — `1` skips atn-server launch and posts against
#                         $ATN_DEMO_URL (default http://localhost:7500).
#   ATN_DEMO_URL        — Base URL of the server.

set -euo pipefail

script_dir="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"
repo_root="$(cd "$script_dir/../.." && pwd)"

url="${ATN_DEMO_URL:-http://localhost:7500}"
server_pid=""

if [ "${ATN_DEMO_REAL:-0}" = "1" ]; then
    coordinator_agent="claude"
    worker_agent="opencode"
    echo "demo: ATN_DEMO_REAL=1 — using real claude / opencode on PATH"
else
    export PATH="$repo_root/tools:$PATH"
    coordinator_agent="fake-claude"
    worker_agent="fake-opencode-glm5"
    echo "demo: using fake shims (fake-claude, fake-opencode-glm5); set ATN_DEMO_REAL=1 for real"
fi

wait_for_ready() {
    for _ in $(seq 1 50); do
        if curl -s --max-time 1 "$url/api/agents" > /dev/null; then
            return 0
        fi
        sleep 0.2
    done
    echo "error: $url/api/agents never responded" >&2
    return 1
}

if [ "${ATN_DEMO_SKIP_BOOT:-0}" != "1" ]; then
    if curl -s --max-time 1 "$url/api/agents" > /dev/null; then
        echo "demo: $url already reachable — reusing running server"
    else
        echo "demo: booting atn-server (logs at /tmp/atn-claude-opencode.log)"
        (
            cd "$repo_root"
            ./target/debug/atn-server agents.toml
        ) > /tmp/atn-claude-opencode.log 2>&1 &
        server_pid=$!
        trap 'if [ -n "$server_pid" ]; then echo "demo: stopping server pid=$server_pid"; kill "$server_pid" 2>/dev/null || true; fi' EXIT
    fi
fi

wait_for_ready
echo "demo: server ready at $url"

post_agent() {
    local name="$1" role="$2" agent_bin="$3"
    local body
    body=$(cat <<EOF
{
    "name": "$name",
    "role": "$role",
    "transport": "local",
    "working_dir": ".",
    "project": "claude-opencode",
    "agent": "$agent_bin"
}
EOF
    )
    local code
    code=$(curl -sS -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' \
        -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
        --data-binary "$body" \
        "$url/api/agents")
    printf '  %-16s %s\n' "$name" "$code"
}

echo "demo: spawning 4 agents..."
post_agent coord       coordinator "$coordinator_agent"
post_agent worker-1    worker      "$worker_agent"
post_agent worker-2    worker      "$worker_agent"
post_agent worker-3    worker      "$worker_agent"

echo
echo "demo: 4 agents up. Open $url to watch them."
echo
echo "Try the per-agent Config button to switch a worker from local to mosh,"
echo "change its agent (e.g. fake-codex → fake-claude), or point it at a"
echo "different dir. (See docs/demos-scripts.md Demo 6 for the full"
echo "treemap / pin / keyboard walkthrough.)"
echo
echo "To tear down just these agents (leaves the server running):"
echo "  for id in coord worker-1 worker-2 worker-3; do"
echo "    curl -X DELETE $url/api/agents/\$id"
echo "  done"

if [ -n "$server_pid" ]; then
    echo "demo: Ctrl-C to stop the server this script launched."
    wait "$server_pid"
fi

content.content:
1	# All Together Now — Usage Guide
2	
3	## Prerequisites
4	
5	- Rust 2024 edition (stable with edition = "2024" or nightly)
6	- macOS or Linux
7	- Optional: `claude` / `codex` / `opencode` / `gemini` / ... on PATH for the
8	  CLIs you want ATN to drive
9	- Optional: `mosh` and key-based `ssh` to any remote dev-user accounts you
10	  want to run agents on
11	
12	See also:
13	- [Demo scripts](./demos-scripts.md) — every demo you can run against the current build, grouped by time + prereqs
14	- [Uber use-case](./uber-use-case.md) — the isolation-first multi-agent story
15	- [Three-agent demo walkthrough](./demo-three-agent.md)
16	- [Remote PTY manual test](./remote-pty.md)
17	- [Architecture](./architecture.md)
18	- [Project status](./status.md)
19	
20	## Quick Start (empty-start)
21	
22	ATN boots with zero agents. You compose them through the UI at runtime.
23	
24	```bash
25	cargo build --workspace
26	cargo run -p atn-server          # uses agents.toml in cwd
27	# or
28	cargo run -p atn-server -- path/to/agents.toml
29	```
30	
31	The default `agents.toml` in the repo has no `[[agent]]` entries:
32	
33	```toml
34	[project]
35	name = "all-together-now"
36	log_dir = ".atn/logs"
37	```
38	
39	That's the intended shape. Open http://localhost:7500 — you'll see an empty
40	dashboard with a **+ New Agent** call-to-action. Click it to add your first
41	agent.
42	
43	> **Why no seed agents?** See [uber-use-case.md](./uber-use-case.md) — the
44	> design favors runtime composition and dev-user isolation on remote hosts
45	> over a static `agents.toml`. The legacy seed shape is preserved as
46	> `agents.example.toml` for reference.
47	
48	## Creating Agents — The New Agent Dialog
49	
50	The **+ New Agent** button opens a form that captures a structured spawn
51	specification. Fields:
52	
53	| Field         | Required            | Example                                       |
54	|---------------|---------------------|-----------------------------------------------|
55	| `name`        | yes — unique id     | `worker-hlasm`                                |
56	| `role`        | default: `worker`   | `coordinator` \| `worker` \| `qa` \| `pm`     |
57	| `transport`   | default: `local`    | `local` \| `mosh` \| `ssh`                    |
58	| `user`        | iff transport≠local | `devh1`                                       |
59	| `host`        | iff transport≠local | `queenbee`                                    |
60	| `working_dir` | yes                 | `/home/devh1/work/hlasm` (path on the target) |
61	| `project`     | no (label only)     | `hlasm`                                       |
62	| `agent`       | yes                 | `claude` \| `codex` \| `opencode-z-ai-glm-5`  |
63	| `agent_args`  | no                  | `--resume --model sonnet`                     |
64	
65	A **live preview** renders the composed command as you type:
66	
67	- **local**: `cd <working_dir> && <agent> [agent_args]`
68	- **mosh / ssh**: `<mosh|ssh> <user>@<host> -- tmux new-session -A -s atn-<name> 'cd <working_dir> && <agent> [agent_args]'`
69	
70	The `-A` flag on `tmux new-session` makes the remote session **idempotent**:
71	the first connect creates it, later reconnects re-attach. That's what makes
72	the **Reconnect** control survive network blips (see below).
73	
74	Missing or malformed fields show up inline (`missing: host, user`) and the
75	**Create** button is disabled until the spec validates. Submission is a
76	`POST /api/agents` with the JSON payload — see the API table.
77	
78	## Agent Lifecycle
79	
80	| Control    | Endpoint                              | Effect                                                                                   |
81	|------------|---------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
82	| Create     | `POST /api/agents`                    | Validates spec, composes the shell command, spawns the PTY.                              |
83	| Restart    | `POST /api/agents/{id}/restart`       | Graceful shutdown (Ctrl-C ×2, kill), then respawn. Sends `^C` to whatever is in the PTY. |
84	| Reconnect  | `POST /api/agents/{id}/reconnect`     | **Hard-kills** the local `mosh`/`ssh` child (no Ctrl-C), respawns. For mosh+tmux, this re-attaches to the still-running remote session so in-progress agent work survives. |
85	| Stop       | `POST /api/agents/{id}/stop`          | Shutdown without delete.                                                                 |
86	| Delete     | `DELETE /api/agents/{id}`             | For remote agents: first sends `^B :kill-session Enter` over the PTY so tmux cleans up server-side. Then the usual graceful shutdown.                                       |
87	| Update cfg | `PUT /api/agents/{id}`                | Edit and restart.                                                                        |
88	
89	## Dashboard Views
90	
91	### Agents (default)
92	
93	Treemap layout — one large focus panel + a heat-sized cloud of smaller
94	tiles. See [docs/scale-ui.md](./scale-ui.md) for a hands-on walkthrough
95	with a 21-agent fleet.
96	
97	- **Focus panel** (left, ~40–55% width): the coordinator by default,
98	  else the hottest agent. Shows name, role, saga step badge, state,
99	  and the full xterm terminal.
100	- **Treemap** (right): every other non-pinned agent, tile area
101	  proportional to a smoothed heat score
102	  (bytes/sec EWMA + state multipliers — `awaiting_human_input` and
103	  `error` boost; `disconnected` mutes). Click any tile's header to
104	  promote it into focus; the layout freezes for 5 s so you can read.
105	- **Tiles below a threshold scale** render as a compact variant: name,
106	  role, state badge, inline sparkline of the last 60 s of bytes/sec,
107	  and the last line of output. No xterm DOM in the compact tile.
108	- **Pin row** (above the treemap): up to 6 agents you want always
109	  visible at a stable size. Pinned agents are excluded from heat-sized
110	  tiling. Click the 📌 on a header or press `p` to pin/unpin the
111	  focused agent.
112	- **Filter bar** (above the pin row): `/` focuses the fuzzy-match
113	  input; chips AND across categories (transport / role / state) and
114	  OR within. `group-by: role|project` packs related agents into
115	  labeled bands. A Layouts dropdown saves named snapshots of
116	  `{pins, focus, focus size, filter text, chips, group-by}` to
117	  localStorage.
118	- **Keyboard**: `1..9` focus Nth hottest, `0` coordinator, `f` toggle
119	  focus size, `p` pin/unpin focus, `/` filter, `Esc` clear focus.
120	- **Persistence**: pins, focus, filter, and group-by survive browser
121	  refreshes via `localStorage`.
122	- **Per-agent controls** in the focus panel: input box, Send, Ctrl-C,
123	  Restart, Reconnect, Stop, Delete.
124	
125	All tile resizes are pure CSS `transform: scale()`; xterm renders at
126	fixed 120×40 native geometry and the PTY only resizes when an agent is
127	explicitly focused.
128	
129	### Graph
130	
131	SVG dependency graph. Nodes color-coded by state; edges show blocking
132	relationships.
133	
134	### Saga
135	
136	`.agentrail/` progress with step cards, trajectories, and the "Distill Skill"
137	button for extracting skills from ICRL data.
138	
139	### Wiki
140	
141	Markdown wiki browser with page list, wiki-link navigation, and ETag-based
142	conflict detection. Also reachable standalone at `/wiki`.
143	
144	### Events
145	
146	Inter-agent event log. Two columns: outbound (coordinator→agents) on the
147	left, inbound (agents→coordinator) on the right. Escalation banners surface
148	at the top.
149	
150	## Inter-Agent Communication
151	
152	### Push Event Kinds
153	
154	JSON events written to per-agent outboxes:
155	
156	- `feature_request`, `bug_fix_request`, `completion_notice`,
157	  `blocked_notice`, `needs_info`, `verification_request`
158	
159	### Routing
160	
161	1. **Target known** → delivered to the target agent's inbox and injected as a
162	   prompt into the target's PTY.
163	2. **Target unknown** → escalated to the wiki `Coordination/Requests` page.
164	3. **No target** → broadcast to `Coordination/*`.
165	
166	Events can be submitted via `POST /api/events`.
167	
168	## Wiki Coordination Pages
169	
170	ATN seeds these pages at startup:
171	
172	| Page                      | Purpose                    |
173	|---------------------------|----------------------------|
174	| `Coordination/Goals`      | Project objectives         |
175	| `Coordination/Agents`     | Who is working on what     |
176	| `Coordination/Requests`   | Open requests between agents |
177	| `Coordination/Blockers`   | Dependency blockers         |
178	| `Coordination/Log`        | Timestamped event log       |
179	
180	## Environment
181	
182	| Variable            | Default       | Purpose                                                    |
183	|---------------------|---------------|------------------------------------------------------------|
184	| `ATN_PORT`          | `7500`        | Bind port for the HTTP/SSE server. `0` picks a free port.  |
185	| `RUST_LOG`          | `atn=info`    | Tracing filter.                                            |
186	| `ATN_DEMO_REAL`     | unset         | In `demos/three-agent/setup.sh`: `1` uses real agent CLIs instead of `tools/fake-*`. |
187	| `ATN_DEMO_SKIP_BOOT`| unset         | In `demos/three-agent/setup.sh`: `1` reuses a running server at `ATN_DEMO_URL`. |
188	| `ATN_DEMO_URL`      | `http://localhost:7500` | Base URL used by the demo script.                  |
189	
190	When the server binds, it prints a machine-readable line:
191	
192	```
193	atn-server ready on 0.0.0.0:<port>
194	```
195	
196	Test harnesses (including `crates/atn-server/tests/three_agent_demo.rs`)
197	parse this to discover the OS-assigned port when `ATN_PORT=0`.
198	
199	## Graceful Shutdown
200	
201	SIGINT (Ctrl-C) and SIGTERM both:
202	
203	1. Stop accepting new HTTP connections
204	2. Send Ctrl-C ×2 to each agent PTY
205	3. Force-kill any remaining child processes
206	4. Log status and exit cleanly
207	
208	## Config Hot-Reload
209	
210	The server watches `agents.toml` (debounced 500ms). Adding or removing
211	`[[agent]]` entries spawns / shuts down agents automatically. Agents created
212	via the New Agent dialog are **not** persisted to `agents.toml` unless you
213	click **Save** in the header (`POST /api/agents/save`).
214	
215	## REST API Reference
216	
217	| Method | Path                              | Description                              |
218	|--------|-----------------------------------|------------------------------------------|
219	| GET    | `/api/agents`                     | List agents with state, spec, launch_command |
220	| POST   | `/api/agents`                     | Create agent from a `SpawnSpec`          |
221	| PUT    | `/api/agents/{id}`                | Update agent config and restart          |
222	| DELETE | `/api/agents/{id}`                | Shut down and remove (tmux-aware)        |
223	| POST   | `/api/agents/{id}/restart`        | Ctrl-C + kill + respawn                  |
224	| POST   | `/api/agents/{id}/reconnect`      | Hard-kill local mosh/ssh + respawn (re-attach) |
225	| POST   | `/api/agents/{id}/stop`           | Shutdown without delete                  |
226	| GET    | `/api/agents/{id}/sse`            | SSE stream of terminal output            |
227	| POST   | `/api/agents/{id}/input`          | Send text or raw bytes                   |
228	| POST   | `/api/agents/{id}/ctrl-c`         | Shortcut for raw `0x03`                  |
229	| POST   | `/api/agents/{id}/resize`         | Update PTY rows/cols from the browser    |
230	| GET    | `/api/agents/{id}/state`          | State snapshot                           |
231	| POST   | `/api/agents/save`                | Persist runtime agents back to `agents.toml` |
232	| GET    | `/api/agents/graph`               | Dependency graph data                    |
233	| GET    | `/api/saga`                       | Project saga progress                    |
234	| POST   | `/api/saga/distill`               | Distill skills from trajectories         |
235	| GET    | `/api/agents/{id}/saga`           | Per-agent saga                           |
236	| GET    | `/api/events`                     | Event log (`?since=N`)                   |
237	| POST   | `/api/events`                     | Submit a `PushEvent`                     |
238	| GET    | `/api/wiki`                       | List wiki pages                          |
239	| GET    | `/api/wiki/{title}`               | Get page (JSON + ETag)                   |
240	| PUT    | `/api/wiki/{title}`               | Create/update page (`If-Match`)          |
241	| PATCH  | `/api/wiki/{title}`               | Structured patch ops                     |
242	| DELETE | `/api/wiki/{title}`               | Delete page (`If-Match`)                 |
243	| GET    | `/wiki`                           | Standalone wiki HTML                     |
244	| GET    | `/wiki/{title}`                   | Standalone wiki page                     |
245	
246	## Logs & Transcripts
247

# All Together Now — Usage Guide

## Prerequisites

- Rust 2024 edition (stable with edition = "2024" or nightly)
- macOS or Linux
- Optional: `claude` / `codex` / `opencode` / `gemini` / ... on PATH for the
  CLIs you want ATN to drive
- Optional: `mosh` and key-based `ssh` to any remote dev-user accounts you
  want to run agents on

See also:
- [Demo scripts](./demos-scripts.md) — every demo you can run against the current build, grouped by time + prereqs
- [Uber use-case](./uber-use-case.md) — the isolation-first multi-agent story
- [Three-agent demo walkthrough](./demo-three-agent.md)
- [Remote PTY manual test](./remote-pty.md)
- [Architecture](./architecture.md)
- [Project status](./status.md)

## Quick Start (empty-start)

ATN boots with zero agents. You compose them through the UI at runtime.

```bash
cargo build --workspace
cargo run -p atn-server          # uses agents.toml in cwd
# or
cargo run -p atn-server -- path/to/agents.toml
```

The default `agents.toml` in the repo has no `[[agent]]` entries:

```toml
[project]
name = "all-together-now"
log_dir = ".atn/logs"
```

That's the intended shape. Open http://localhost:7500 — you'll see an empty
dashboard with a **+ New Agent** call-to-action. Click it to add your first
agent.

> **Why no seed agents?** See [uber-use-case.md](./uber-use-case.md) — the
> design favors runtime composition and dev-user isolation on remote hosts
> over a static `agents.toml`. The legacy seed shape is preserved as
> `agents.example.toml` for reference.

## Creating Agents — The New Agent Dialog

The **+ New Agent** button opens a form that captures a structured spawn
specification. Fields:

| Field         | Required            | Example                                       |
|---------------|---------------------|-----------------------------------------------|
| `name`        | yes — unique id     | `worker-hlasm`                                |
| `role`        | default: `worker`   | `coordinator` \| `worker` \| `qa` \| `pm`     |
| `transport`   | default: `local`    | `local` \| `mosh` \| `ssh`                    |
| `user`        | iff transport≠local | `devh1`                                       |
| `host`        | iff transport≠local | `queenbee`                                    |
| `working_dir` | yes                 | `/home/devh1/work/hlasm` (path on the target) |
| `project`     | no (label only)     | `hlasm`                                       |
| `agent`       | yes                 | `claude` \| `codex` \| `opencode-z-ai-glm-5`  |
| `agent_args`  | no                  | `--resume --model sonnet`                     |

A **live preview** renders the composed command as you type:

- **local**: `cd <working_dir> && <agent> [agent_args]`
- **mosh / ssh**: `<mosh|ssh> <user>@<host> -- tmux new-session -A -s atn-<name> 'cd <working_dir> && <agent> [agent_args]'`

The `-A` flag on `tmux new-session` makes the remote session **idempotent**:
the first connect creates it, later reconnects re-attach. That's what makes
the **Reconnect** control survive network blips (see below).

Missing or malformed fields show up inline (`missing: host, user`) and the
**Create** button is disabled until the spec validates. Submission is a
`POST /api/agents` with the JSON payload — see the API table.

## Agent Lifecycle

| Control    | Endpoint                              | Effect                                                                                   |
|------------|---------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Create     | `POST /api/agents`                    | Validates spec, composes the shell command, spawns the PTY.                              |
| Restart    | `POST /api/agents/{id}/restart`       | Graceful shutdown (Ctrl-C ×2, kill), then respawn. Sends `^C` to whatever is in the PTY. |
| Reconnect  | `POST /api/agents/{id}/reconnect`     | **Hard-kills** the local `mosh`/`ssh` child (no Ctrl-C), respawns. For mosh+tmux, this re-attaches to the still-running remote session so in-progress agent work survives. |
| Stop       | `POST /api/agents/{id}/stop`          | Shutdown without delete.                                                                 |
| Delete     | `DELETE /api/agents/{id}`             | For remote agents: first sends `^B :kill-session Enter` over the PTY so tmux cleans up server-side. Then the usual graceful shutdown.                                       |
| Update cfg | `PUT /api/agents/{id}`                | Edit and restart.                                                                        |

## Dashboard Views

### Agents (default)

Treemap layout — one large focus panel + a heat-sized cloud of smaller
tiles. See [docs/scale-ui.md](./scale-ui.md) for a hands-on walkthrough
with a 21-agent fleet.

- **Focus panel** (left, ~40–55% width): the coordinator by default,
  else the hottest agent. Shows name, role, saga step badge, state,
  and the full xterm terminal.
- **Treemap** (right): every other non-pinned agent, tile area
  proportional to a smoothed heat score
  (bytes/sec EWMA + state multipliers — `awaiting_human_input` and
  `error` boost; `disconnected` mutes). Click any tile's header to
  promote it into focus; the layout freezes for 5 s so you can read.
- **Tiles below a threshold scale** render as a compact variant: name,
  role, state badge, inline sparkline of the last 60 s of bytes/sec,
  and the last line of output. No xterm DOM in the compact tile.
- **Pin row** (above the treemap): up to 6 agents you want always
  visible at a stable size. Pinned agents are excluded from heat-sized
  tiling. Click the 📌 on a header or press `p` to pin/unpin the
  focused agent.
- **Filter bar** (above the pin row): `/` focuses the fuzzy-match
  input; chips AND across categories (transport / role / state) and
  OR within. `group-by: role|project` packs related agents into
  labeled bands. A Layouts dropdown saves named snapshots of
  `{pins, focus, focus size, filter text, chips, group-by}` to
  localStorage.
- **Keyboard**: `1..9` focus Nth hottest, `0` coordinator, `f` toggle
  focus size, `p` pin/unpin focus, `/` filter, `Esc` clear focus.
- **Persistence**: pins, focus, filter, and group-by survive browser
  refreshes via `localStorage`.
- **Per-agent controls** in the focus panel: input box, Send, Ctrl-C,
  Restart, Reconnect, Stop, Delete.

All tile resizes are pure CSS `transform: scale()`; xterm renders at
fixed 120×40 native geometry and the PTY only resizes when an agent is
explicitly focused.

### Graph

SVG dependency graph. Nodes color-coded by state; edges show blocking
relationships.

### Saga

`.agentrail/` progress with step cards, trajectories, and the "Distill Skill"
button for extracting skills from ICRL data.

### Wiki

Markdown wiki browser with page list, wiki-link navigation, and ETag-based
conflict detection. Also reachable standalone at `/wiki`.

### Events

Inter-agent event log. Two columns: outbound (coordinator→agents) on the
left, inbound (agents→coordinator) on the right. Escalation banners surface
at the top.

## Inter-Agent Communication

### Push Event Kinds

JSON events written to per-agent outboxes:

- `feature_request`, `bug_fix_request`, `completion_notice`,
  `blocked_notice`, `needs_info`, `verification_request`

### Routing

1. **Target known** → delivered to the target agent's inbox and injected as a
   prompt into the target's PTY.
2. **Target unknown** → escalated to the wiki `Coordination/Requests` page.
3. **No target** → broadcast to `Coordination/*`.

Events can be submitted via `POST /api/events`.

## Wiki Coordination Pages

ATN seeds these pages at startup:

| Page                      | Purpose                    |
|---------------------------|----------------------------|
| `Coordination/Goals`      | Project objectives         |
| `Coordination/Agents`     | Who is working on what     |
| `Coordination/Requests`   | Open requests between agents |
| `Coordination/Blockers`   | Dependency blockers         |
| `Coordination/Log`        | Timestamped event log       |

## Environment

| Variable            | Default       | Purpose                                                    |
|---------------------|---------------|------------------------------------------------------------|
| `ATN_PORT`          | `7500`        | Bind port for the HTTP/SSE server. `0` picks a free port.  |
| `RUST_LOG`          | `atn=info`    | Tracing filter.                                            |
| `ATN_DEMO_REAL`     | unset         | In `demos/three-agent/setup.sh`: `1` uses real agent CLIs instead of `tools/fake-*`. |
| `ATN_DEMO_SKIP_BOOT`| unset         | In `demos/three-agent/setup.sh`: `1` reuses a running server at `ATN_DEMO_URL`. |
| `ATN_DEMO_URL`      | `http://localhost:7500` | Base URL used by the demo script.                  |

When the server binds, it prints a machine-readable line:

```
atn-server ready on 0.0.0.0:<port>
```

Test harnesses (including `crates/atn-server/tests/three_agent_demo.rs`)
parse this to discover the OS-assigned port when `ATN_PORT=0`.

## Graceful Shutdown

SIGINT (Ctrl-C) and SIGTERM both:

1. Stop accepting new HTTP connections
2. Send Ctrl-C ×2 to each agent PTY
3. Force-kill any remaining child processes
4. Log status and exit cleanly

## Config Hot-Reload

The server watches `agents.toml` (debounced 500ms). Adding or removing
`[[agent]]` entries spawns / shuts down agents automatically. Agents created
via the New Agent dialog are **not** persisted to `agents.toml` unless you
click **Save** in the header (`POST /api/agents/save`).

## REST API Reference

| Method | Path                              | Description                              |
|--------|-----------------------------------|------------------------------------------|
| GET    | `/api/agents`                     | List agents with state, spec, launch_command |
| POST   | `/api/agents`                     | Create agent from a `SpawnSpec`          |
| PUT    | `/api/agents/{id}`                | Update agent config and restart          |
| DELETE | `/api/agents/{id}`                | Shut down and remove (tmux-aware)        |
| POST   | `/api/agents/{id}/restart`        | Ctrl-C + kill + respawn                  |
| POST   | `/api/agents/{id}/reconnect`      | Hard-kill local mosh/ssh + respawn (re-attach) |
| POST   | `/api/agents/{id}/stop`           | Shutdown without delete                  |
| GET    | `/api/agents/{id}/sse`            | SSE stream of terminal output            |
| POST   | `/api/agents/{id}/input`          | Send text or raw bytes                   |
| POST   | `/api/agents/{id}/ctrl-c`         | Shortcut for raw `0x03`                  |
| POST   | `/api/agents/{id}/resize`         | Update PTY rows/cols from the browser    |
| GET    | `/api/agents/{id}/state`          | State snapshot                           |
| POST   | `/api/agents/save`                | Persist runtime agents back to `agents.toml` |
| GET    | `/api/agents/graph`               | Dependency graph data                    |
| GET    | `/api/saga`                       | Project saga progress                    |
| POST   | `/api/saga/distill`               | Distill skills from trajectories         |
| GET    | `/api/agents/{id}/saga`           | Per-agent saga                           |
| GET    | `/api/events`                     | Event log (`?since=N`)                   |
| POST   | `/api/events`                     | Submit a `PushEvent`                     |
| GET    | `/api/wiki`                       | List wiki pages                          |
| GET    | `/api/wiki/{title}`               | Get page (JSON + ETag)                   |
| PUT    | `/api/wiki/{title}`               | Create/update page (`If-Match`)          |
| PATCH  | `/api/wiki/{title}`               | Structured patch ops                     |
| DELETE | `/api/wiki/{title}`               | Delete page (`If-Match`)                 |
| GET    | `/wiki`                           | Standalone wiki HTML                     |
| GET    | `/wiki/{title}`                   | Standalone wiki page                     |

## Logs & Transcripts

Per-agent files in `{log_dir}/{agent_id}/`:

- `transcript.log` — raw PTY bytes (replayable via `atn-replay`)
- `inputs.jsonl` — timestamped input events
- `events.jsonl` — structured signals (`prompt_ready`, `idle_detected`,
  `push_event`, `disconnected`)

## The Three-Agent Demo

Shortest path to seeing ATN coordinate multiple agents end-to-end:

```bash
# Runs against fake agent shims — no real CLIs required.
./demos/three-agent/setup.sh
```

See [docs/demo-three-agent.md](./demo-three-agent.md) for the full
walkthrough, including how to swap in real `claude` / `codex` /
`opencode-z-ai-glm-5` via `ATN_DEMO_REAL=1`.

# All Together Now — Status

## Current State: Remote-Agent Demo Saga Complete

Phases 0–8 shipped plus the follow-on **remote-agent demo saga** (5 steps,
all shipped in this sequence):

1. **empty-start** — ATN boots with zero agents; UI renders empty state
   with a + New Agent CTA. `agents.toml` defaults to `[project]`-only; seed
   preserved as `agents.example.toml`.
2. **new-agent-dialog** — structured `SpawnSpec` (name/role/transport/
   host/user/working_dir/project/agent/agent_args) in `atn-core`; `POST
   /api/agents` validates and composes shell commands for local/mosh/ssh.
   Both the Yew modal and the static HTML form use the same schema.
3. **remote-pty-transport** — `OutputSignal::Disconnected` signal pipes
   PTY EOF → `AgentState::Disconnected`; `POST /api/agents/{id}/reconnect`
   re-attaches to remote tmux; graceful delete sends `^B :kill-session` to
   clean up tmux server-side. `tools/fake-mosh` + symlinks back the
   integration tests.
4. **three-agent-demo** — `tools/fake-claude`, `tools/fake-codex`,
   `tools/fake-opencode-glm5` shims; `demos/three-agent/fixtures/*.json`
   + `setup.sh` (`ATN_DEMO_REAL=1` for real CLIs); `ATN_PORT` env +
   `atn-server ready on <addr>` stdout marker; `crates/atn-server/tests/
   three_agent_demo.rs` end-to-end integration test.
5. **docs-refresh** — this doc + `docs/usage.md`, `docs/demo-three-agent.md`,
   `docs/uber-use-case.md` cross-links, README quickstart.

End-to-end multi-agent demo working with reg-rs regression test.

## What Exists

| Crate | Status | Description |
|-------|--------|-------------|
| `atn-core` | Complete | Domain types: agent, event, inbox, error, config, router |
| `atn-pty` | Complete | PTY session management, reader, writer (with input logging), state tracker, transcript |
| `atn-server` | Complete | Axum HTTP/SSE server with all REST endpoints + agent CRUD |
| `atn-wiki` | Complete | FileWikiStorage + coordination page seeding |
| `atn-trail` | Complete | Agentrail file reader + CLI wrapper |
| `atn-replay` | Complete | PTY transcript viewer: screenshot, dashboard (org-mode), HTML output |
| `atn-ui` | Placeholder | Yew frontend (server uses embedded static HTML) |

## Phase Summary

| Phase | Description | Status |
|-------|-------------|--------|
| 0 | Project skeleton, workspace, core types | Done |
| 1 | Single agent PTY with integration tests | Done |
| 2 | Minimal web UI with xterm.js + SSE | Done |
| 3 | Multi-agent management with dashboard grid | Done |
| 4 | Wiki integration with REST API + CAS | Done |
| 5 | Message routing with outbox polling | Done |
| 6 | Agentrail integration with saga UI | Done |
| 7 | Polish: shutdown, restart, graph, notifications, hot-reload | Done |
| 8 | Session management UI (agent CRUD from browser) | Done |
| — | Demo, input logging, atn-replay crate | Done |
| R1 | Empty-start: boot with zero agents | Done |
| R2 | New Agent dialog: structured SpawnSpec + compose | Done |
| R3 | Remote PTY transport: reconnect + tmux cleanup | Done |
| R4 | Three-agent demo: fake shims + integration test | Done |
| R5 | Docs refresh: usage, demo walkthrough, cross-links | Done |
| S1..S5 | Scale-UI saga: heat score, compact tile, CSS-scaled xterm, treemap, pin + keyboard | Done |
| S6 | Demo scripts: docs/demos-scripts.md menu of runnable demos | Done |
| S7 | Search + chips + group-by + saved layouts | Done |
| S8 | Scale-UI fleet (21 fake agents) + docs/scale-ui.md walkthrough | Done |

## Demo

Two AI agents collaborate via ATN to build a CLI app:
1. Dev agent creates `app.py` with a `greet` command (via opencode + deepseek)
2. ATN routes a feature request to feature agent
3. Feature agent reads `app.py` and adds a `farewell` command
4. Both commands verified working; completion notice routed back

Run: `bash demo/run-demo.sh` (~55 seconds, ~$0.006 API cost)
Capture artifacts: `ATN_CAPTURE_DIR=demo/last-run bash demo/run-demo.sh`
Regression test: `REG_RS_DATA_DIR=work/reg-rs reg-rs run -p atn-demo`

## Replay Tooling

`atn-replay` renders PTY transcripts from `.atn/logs/*/transcript.log`:

```
atn-replay dashboard .atn/logs -o dashboard.org   # emacs auto-revert
atn-replay screenshot transcript.log              # text box to stdout
atn-replay screenshot transcript.log --html f.html # standalone HTML
atn-replay screenshot transcript.log --html-fragment f.html  # org embed
atn-replay list .atn/logs                         # list agents + sizes
```

## Logging

Three log files per agent in `.atn/logs/{agent_id}/`:
- `transcript.log` — raw PTY output bytes (replayable via atn-replay)
- `inputs.jsonl` — timestamped input commands (human_text, coordinator_command, etc.)
- `events.jsonl` — state transitions (prompt_ready, idle_detected, push_event)

## Architecture

Cargo workspace with 7 crates (library-first design):
- **atn-core**: pure domain types, no async, no I/O
- **atn-pty**: PTY session lifecycle via portable-pty + tokio
- **atn-server**: Axum binary with REST + SSE + embedded static UI
- **atn-wiki**: wiki storage and coordination logic
- **atn-trail**: agentrail file reader and CLI wrapper
- **atn-replay**: PTY transcript rendering (vt100 + clap)
- **atn-ui**: Yew components (placeholder)

## Known Issues

- ATN notification `(priority: High)` causes bash syntax error (unquoted parentheses)
- State tracking timing is fragile for programmatic task completion detection

## Upstream Projects

### agentrail-rs
**Location**: `~/github/sw-vibe-coding/agentrail-rs`
**Integration**: CLI binary in PATH, invoked by `atn-trail/src/cli.rs`

### wiki-rs
**Location**: `~/github/sw-vibe-coding/wiki-rs`
**Integration**: `wiki-common` crate used as path dependency (with `server` feature)

## Quality

- Zero clippy warnings (`cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings`)
- All tests passing (`cargo test --workspace`)
- Rust 2024 edition throughout

# ATN Demo Scripts

Every demo you can run against the current build (through scale-UI step 5).
Each section is self-contained — pick the one that fits your audience and
time window.

## Prerequisites shared by every demo

- Build the workspace once:
  ```bash
  cargo build --workspace
  ```
- The fake agent shims in `tools/` are already executable. If you want
  them on `$PATH` (used by the three-agent demo script), export:
  ```bash
  export PATH="$(pwd)/tools:$PATH"
  ```
- The server's default port is 7500. Override with `ATN_PORT` if 7500 is
  already in use on your machine.

## At-a-glance index

| # | Demo                                    | Duration | Needs real rack host? |
|---|-----------------------------------------|----------|-----------------------|
| 1 | Empty-start + New Agent dialog (local)  | 3 min    | No                    |
| 2 | New Agent dialog preview — mosh/ssh     | 2 min    | No                    |
| 3 | Three-agent topology (fake shims)       | 4 min    | No                    |
| 4 | Reconnect after mosh drop               | 5 min    | **Yes** (or substitute) |
| 5 | Graceful delete clears remote tmux      | 2 min    | **Yes**               |
| 6 | Treemap scale-UI (heat + focus + pin + kbd) | 6 min | No                    |
| 7 | REST API tour                           | 4 min    | No                    |
| 8 | Scale-UI fleet (21 fake agents)         | 5–15 min | No                    |

What is **not** demoable yet: the Ollama / CUDA transports (future
sagas). Everything else in the scale-UI saga (search, chips, grouping,
saved layouts, the 21-agent fleet) is shipped.

---

## Demo 1 — Empty start + New Agent dialog (local)

**What it shows**
- ATN boots with zero agents; the dashboard renders a deliberate empty
  state with a `+ New Agent` call-to-action.
- The dialog captures a structured `SpawnSpec` (not a free-form shell
  command) and renders a live preview of the composed shell line.

**Why it matters**
- The dialog is the safest way to spawn an agent: fields are validated
  per transport, forbidden characters are rejected, and the exact
  command ATN types into the PTY is visible before you click Create.

**Setup**
```bash
cargo run -p atn-server
```
Open http://localhost:7500.

**Steps**
1. Dashboard shows **No agents yet** + a primary **+ New Agent** button.
2. Click **+ New Agent**. Fill:
   - `name`: `demo-1`
   - `role`: `worker`
   - `transport`: `local`
   - `working_dir`: `.`
   - `agent`: `bash`
3. Watch the preview line update as you type: `cd . && bash`.
4. Click **Create**. A terminal panel appears with a live bash shell.
5. Hover the agent name in the header — tooltip shows the same composed
   command ATN typed into the PTY.

**Cleanup**
Click the agent's **Delete** control, or:
```bash
curl -X DELETE http://localhost:7500/api/agents/demo-1
```

**Variations**
- Create a second agent with `agent=fake-claude` to see the banner a
  real LLM CLI would emit on startup.
- Try invalid input (blank name) — the **Create** button stays disabled
  and the missing-field list appears.

---

## Demo 2 — New Agent dialog, mosh/ssh preview

**What it shows**
- Switching `transport` to `mosh` or `ssh` instantly rewrites the
  preview to the full pipeline:
  ```
  mosh <user>@<host> -- tmux new-session -A -s atn-<name> 'cd <dir> && <agent>'
  ```
- The `-A` flag is how reconnect (Demo 4) re-attaches without losing
  work.

**Why it matters**
- Audience sees, at dialog time, the exact tmux pipeline. Everyone
  understands what will run and where.

**Setup**
Same as Demo 1. Don't click Create — this demo is about the preview.

**Steps**
1. Click **+ New Agent**.
2. Switch `transport` to `mosh`. Notice `user`/`host` become required.
3. Fill: `name: worker-hlasm`, `user: devh1`, `host: queenbee`,
   `working_dir: /home/devh1/work/hlasm`, `agent: codex`.
4. Preview reads:
   ```
   mosh devh1@queenbee -- tmux new-session -A -s atn-worker-hlasm \
     'cd /home/devh1/work/hlasm && codex'
   ```
5. Switch `transport` to `ssh` — the binary flips but everything else
   is identical.
6. Click **Cancel**.

**Cleanup**
Nothing to clean — no agent was created.

272	## Demo 6 — Treemap scale-UI
273	
274	**What it shows** (scale-UI saga, steps 1–5)
275	- Per-agent **heat** score (EWMA of bytes-per-second + state
276	  multipliers) drives tile area in a squarified treemap.
277	- Focus panel auto-picks (coordinator first, else highest heat) and
278	  occupies 40–70% of viewport width.
279	- Click any tile's header or a compact tile to swap it into focus;
280	  layout freezes for 5 s so you can read.
281	- Xterm renders at fixed native geometry (120×40); tile resizing is
282	  pure CSS `transform: scale()`. PTY is never resized by layout
283	  churn — only an explicit focus.
284	- Pin row holds up to 6 always-visible mini tiles; pinned agents skip
285	  the treemap.
286	- Keyboard: `1..9` focus Nth hottest, `0` coordinator, `f` toggle
287	  focus width, `p` pin/unpin, `Esc` clear focus, `/` (filter input,
288	  stub until step 7 lands).
289	- Pins + focus + focus width persist in localStorage across refreshes.
290	
291	**Why it matters**
292	- The dashboard scales from 3 to ~30 agents without the grid turning
293	  into unreadable micro-panels. Hot agents naturally command attention.
294	
295	**Setup**
296	```bash
297	cargo run -p atn-server
298	```
299	Open http://localhost:7500. No agents yet.
300	
301	**Steps — spawn a mix by hand**
302	1. Create 6–10 agents via the dialog. Use a mix of transports (all
303	   `local` is fine for a demo) and agents:
304	   - 1 coordinator / `fake-claude`
305	   - 3 workers / `fake-claude` (quiet)
306	   - 2 workers / `bash` (you can poke them to pump bytes)
307	   - 2 workers / `fake-codex` (quiet)
308	2. Observe: coordinator auto-picks focus (big left panel); workers
309	   tile to the right proportional to (very small) heat.
310	3. Click into one of the bash agents' input box and send several
311	   lines — watch its tile grow in the treemap as bytes/sec climbs.
312	   After 5 s of quiet, it shrinks again.
313	4. Hover an agent name — tooltip shows the composed launch command.
314	5. Click a non-focus tile's header → it swaps to focus; layout
315	   freezes 5 s.
316	6. Press `1` — the Nth hottest tile is focused.
317	7. Press `p` — the current focus is pinned (📌 on header, moves to
318	   pin row, focus returns to auto-pick).
319	8. Press `f` — focus panel toggles medium (40–50%) ↔ large (55–70%).
320	9. Press `Esc` — focus clears; auto-pick takes over on the next tick.
321	10. Refresh the page — pins and focus are restored from localStorage.
322	
323	**Setup — scripted mix (faster)**
324	The three-agent script (Demo 3) plus a couple of extras gets you to
325	~5 agents quickly:
326	```bash
327	./demos/three-agent/setup.sh
328	# In another shell, add a few more:
329	for i in 1 2 3; do
330	  curl -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
331	    -d "{\"name\":\"extra-$i\",\"transport\":\"local\",\"working_dir\":\".\",\"agent\":\"fake-claude\"}" \
332	    http://localhost:7500/api/agents
333	done
334	```
335	
336	**Cleanup**
337	```bash
338	for id in $(curl -s http://localhost:7500/api/agents | python3 -c \
339	  "import json,sys;print(' '.join(a['id'] for a in json.load(sys.stdin)))"); do
340	  curl -X DELETE http://localhost:7500/api/agents/$id
341	done
342	```
343	
344	**Variations**
345	- Run the fake-claude agents with `pump-input` loops to keep their
346	  heat up:
347	  ```bash
348	  for i in $(seq 1 50); do
349	    curl -sS -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
350	      -d "{\"text\":\"ping $i\"}" \
351	      http://localhost:7500/api/agents/extra-1/input
352	    sleep 0.3
353	  done
354	  ```
355	- Check raw numbers via `GET /api/agents/heat`.
356	
357	---

362	- Every UI interaction is backed by a REST endpoint. Scripts and bots

364

368	
369	**Setup**

371	cargo run -p atn-server
372	```
373	
374	**Steps**
375	```bash
376	# List agents (empty).
377	curl -s http://localhost:7500/api/agents
378	
379	# Create one from a SpawnSpec.

## Demo 6 — Treemap scale-UI

**What it shows** (scale-UI saga, steps 1–5)
- Per-agent **heat** score (EWMA of bytes-per-second + state
  multipliers) drives tile area in a squarified treemap.
- Focus panel auto-picks (coordinator first, else highest heat) and
  occupies 40–70% of viewport width.
- Click any tile's header or a compact tile to swap it into focus;
  layout freezes for 5 s so you can read.
- Xterm renders at fixed native geometry (120×40); tile resizing is
  pure CSS `transform: scale()`. PTY is never resized by layout
  churn — only an explicit focus.
- Pin row holds up to 6 always-visible mini tiles; pinned agents skip
  the treemap.
- Keyboard: `1..9` focus Nth hottest, `0` coordinator, `f` toggle
  focus width, `p` pin/unpin, `Esc` clear focus, `/` (filter input,
  stub until step 7 lands).
- Pins + focus + focus width persist in localStorage across refreshes.

**Why it matters**
- The dashboard scales from 3 to ~30 agents without the grid turning
  into unreadable micro-panels. Hot agents naturally command attention.

**Setup**
```bash
cargo run -p atn-server
```
Open http://localhost:7500. No agents yet.

**Steps — spawn a mix by hand**
1. Create 6–10 agents via the dialog. Use a mix of transports (all
   `local` is fine for a demo) and agents:
   - 1 coordinator / `fake-claude`
   - 3 workers / `fake-claude` (quiet)
   - 2 workers / `bash` (you can poke them to pump bytes)
   - 2 workers / `fake-codex` (quiet)
2. Observe: coordinator auto-picks focus (big left panel); workers
   tile to the right proportional to (very small) heat.
3. Click into one of the bash agents' input box and send several
   lines — watch its tile grow in the treemap as bytes/sec climbs.
   After 5 s of quiet, it shrinks again.
4. Hover an agent name — tooltip shows the composed launch command.
5. Click a non-focus tile's header → it swaps to focus; layout
   freezes 5 s.
6. Press `1` — the Nth hottest tile is focused.
7. Press `p` — the current focus is pinned (📌 on header, moves to
   pin row, focus returns to auto-pick).
8. Press `f` — focus panel toggles medium (40–50%) ↔ large (55–70%).
9. Press `Esc` — focus clears; auto-pick takes over on the next tick.
10. Refresh the page — pins and focus are restored from localStorage.

**Setup — scripted mix (faster)**
The three-agent script (Demo 3) plus a couple of extras gets you to
~5 agents quickly:
```bash
./demos/three-agent/setup.sh
# In another shell, add a few more:
for i in 1 2 3; do
  curl -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
    -d "{\"name\":\"extra-$i\",\"transport\":\"local\",\"working_dir\":\".\",\"agent\":\"fake-claude\"}" \
    http://localhost:7500/api/agents
done
```

**Cleanup**
```bash
for id in $(curl -s http://localhost:7500/api/agents | python3 -c \
  "import json,sys;print(' '.join(a['id'] for a in json.load(sys.stdin)))"); do
  curl -X DELETE http://localhost:7500/api/agents/$id
done
```

**Variations**
- Run the fake-claude agents with `pump-input` loops to keep their
  heat up:
  ```bash
  for i in $(seq 1 50); do
    curl -sS -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
      -d "{\"text\":\"ping $i\"}" \
      http://localhost:7500/api/agents/extra-1/input
    sleep 0.3
  done
  ```
- Check raw numbers via `GET /api/agents/heat`.

425	## Demo 8 — Scale-UI fleet (21 fake agents)
426	
427	**What it shows**
428	- Everything scale-UI saga steps 1–7: heat-sized treemap, click-to-focus
429	  with 5 s freeze, compact tiles below threshold, pin row, keyboard
430	  shortcuts, `/` filter + transport/role/state chips, group-by-role
431	  packing, saved layouts, localStorage persistence.
432	- Five distinct activity profiles via `tools/fake-agent-profile`:
433	  spammer (hot), quiet (tiny), periodic (pulsing), awaiting-input
434	  (state-boosted), error (5 s then Disconnected).
435	
436	**Why it matters**
437	- Single command stands up a realistic fleet; the treemap earns its
438	  keep at 20+ agents where the old cols-N grid ran out of room.
439	
440	**Setup**
441	```bash
442	./demos/scale/setup.sh
443	```
444	The script boots (or reuses) `atn-server` on :7500 and POSTs 21 agents
445	(1 coordinator + 4 spammers + 8 quiet + 4 periodic + 2 awaiting + 2
446	error).
447	
448	**Steps**
449	See [docs/scale-ui.md](./scale-ui.md) for the full guided walkthrough
450	(8 exercises: click-to-focus, pin row, 1–9 keyboard, `/` filter, chip
451	interactions, group-by, saved layouts, disconnect visibility). The
452	short version:
453	
454	1. Open http://localhost:7500 — treemap fills in within a few seconds.
455	   Coordinator is focus; spammers dominate the right side.
456	2. Press `1` to focus the hottest spammer. Press `p` to pin it. Press
457	   `Esc` to clear focus.
458	3. Press `/` and type `quiet` — treemap narrows to the 8 quiet agents.
459	4. Toggle **group-by: role** — watch the treemap pack into labeled
460	   clusters.
461	5. Click **Save** under Layouts. Name it `quiet-focus`. Refresh the
462	   browser — your layout comes back.
463	
464	**Cleanup**
465	Ctrl-C the terminal running `setup.sh`, or:
466	```bash
467	for id in coord-main spammer-0{1,2,3,4} quiet-0{1,2,3,4,5,6,7,8} \
468	          periodic-0{1,2,3,4} awaiting-0{1,2} error-0{1,2}; do
469	    curl -X DELETE http://localhost:7500/api/agents/$id
470	done
471	```
472	
473	**Variations**
474	- Edit `demos/scale/setup.sh` to change counts or mix in more
475	  `awaiting-input` agents to see how the state boost dominates the
476	  treemap.
477	
478	---
479	
480	## Picking one for a short slot
481	
482	- **Under 5 min, no infra**: Demo 1 + the preview bit of Demo 2.
483	- **5–10 min, no infra**: Demo 3 + the middle of Demo 6 (type into a
484	  bash agent, watch its tile grow).
485	- **10–15 min, real rack host**: Demos 1 → 2 (preview) → 4 (reconnect)
486	  → 5 (cleanup). That's the full uber-use-case story.
487	- **Integrations / API-minded audience**: Demo 7 + a click-through of
488	  Demo 6.
489	
490	## When new demos arrive
491	
492	- Future Ollama / CUDA / git-sync sagas: each gets its own section here.
493	
494	See also:
495	- [docs/usage.md](./usage.md) — operational guide
496	- [docs/uber-use-case.md](./uber-use-case.md) — design story
497	- [docs/demo-three-agent.md](./demo-three-agent.md) — the three-agent
498	  demo in more depth
499	- [docs/scale-ui.md](./scale-ui.md) — the 21-agent scale-UI walkthrough
500	- [docs/remote-pty.md](./remote-pty.md) — manual remote PTY
501	  walkthrough

**What it shows**
- Everything scale-UI saga steps 1–7: heat-sized treemap, click-to-focus
  with 5 s freeze, compact tiles below threshold, pin row, keyboard
  shortcuts, `/` filter + transport/role/state chips, group-by-role
  packing, saved layouts, localStorage persistence.
- Five distinct activity profiles via `tools/fake-agent-profile`:
  spammer (hot), quiet (tiny), periodic (pulsing), awaiting-input
  (state-boosted), error (5 s then Disconnected).

**Why it matters**
- Single command stands up a realistic fleet; the treemap earns its
  keep at 20+ agents where the old cols-N grid ran out of room.

**Setup**
```bash
./demos/scale/setup.sh
```
The script boots (or reuses) `atn-server` on :7500 and POSTs 21 agents
(1 coordinator + 4 spammers + 8 quiet + 4 periodic + 2 awaiting + 2
error).

**Steps**
See [docs/scale-ui.md](./scale-ui.md) for the full guided walkthrough
(8 exercises: click-to-focus, pin row, 1–9 keyboard, `/` filter, chip
interactions, group-by, saved layouts, disconnect visibility). The
short version:

1. Open http://localhost:7500 — treemap fills in within a few seconds.
   Coordinator is focus; spammers dominate the right side.
2. Press `1` to focus the hottest spammer. Press `p` to pin it. Press
   `Esc` to clear focus.
3. Press `/` and type `quiet` — treemap narrows to the 8 quiet agents.
4. Toggle **group-by: role** — watch the treemap pack into labeled
   clusters.
5. Click **Save** under Layouts. Name it `quiet-focus`. Refresh the
   browser — your layout comes back.

**Cleanup**
Ctrl-C the terminal running `setup.sh`, or:
```bash
for id in coord-main spammer-0{1,2,3,4} quiet-0{1,2,3,4,5,6,7,8} \
          periodic-0{1,2,3,4} awaiting-0{1,2} error-0{1,2}; do
    curl -X DELETE http://localhost:7500/api/agents/$id
done
```

**Variations**
- Edit `demos/scale/setup.sh` to change counts or mix in more
  `awaiting-input` agents to see how the state boost dominates the
  treemap.

---

## Picking one for a short slot

- **Under 5 min, no infra**: Demo 1 + the preview bit of Demo 2.
- **5–10 min, no infra**: Demo 3 + the middle of Demo 6 (type into a
  bash agent, watch its tile grow).
- **10–15 min, real rack host**: Demos 1 → 2 (preview) → 4 (reconnect)
  → 5 (cleanup). That's the full uber-use-case story.
- **Integrations / API-minded audience**: Demo 7 + a click-through of
  Demo 6.

## When new demos arrive

- Future Ollama / CUDA / git-sync sagas: each gets its own section here.

See also:
- [docs/usage.md](./usage.md) — operational guide
- [docs/uber-use-case.md](./uber-use-case.md) — design story
- [docs/demo-three-agent.md](./demo-three-agent.md) — the three-agent
  demo in more depth
- [docs/scale-ui.md](./scale-ui.md) — the 21-agent scale-UI walkthrough
- [docs/remote-pty.md](./remote-pty.md) — manual remote PTY
  walkthrough

# Scale-UI Walkthrough

End-to-end tour of the scale-UI (saga steps 1–7) using a fleet of 21 fake
agents that exercise every part of the treemap. About 15 minutes if you
play through every section; 5 if you just want the money shot.

> Short on time? The quickest demo is
> [docs/demos-scripts.md § Demo 6](./demos-scripts.md#demo-6--treemap-scale-ui)
> with hand-spawned agents. This doc goes deeper using the scripted fleet.

## Prereqs

- `cargo build --workspace` has been run at least once.
- The `tools/` directory is on disk next to the repo root (ATN's
  `demos/scale/setup.sh` prepends it to `PATH` for you).
- A modern browser. No other infrastructure.

## Boot the fleet

```bash
./demos/scale/setup.sh
```

The script:

1. Launches `atn-server` on :7500 (or reuses whatever is there).
2. Waits for `/api/agents` to respond.
3. POSTs **21** agents covering five activity profiles:

   | Count | Name prefix   | Profile        | What it does                                                     |
