Here's the recommended batch order, then I'll queue up batch 1:

### Batch order
1. **`ops-polish`** — shell-escape fix + pty-screenshot + watchdog. Small, foundational, visibility.
2. **`atn-cli`** — Rust CLI replacing curl+jq in demos. Builds on ops-polish because `screenshot` and `events` subcommands land cleaner after the endpoints exist.
3. **`dashboard-polish`** — events-view improvements + wiki side-panel. Visual, can land in parallel with atn-cli but reads better after tooling is in place.
4. **`atn-agent`** — Rust LLM agent wrapper (ollama/opencode). Big unlock; fake-shim-free local demos.
5. **`git-sync-agents`** — distributed PR flow from uber-use-case. Biggest; depends on watchdog + atn-agent for best UX.

Starting saga 1:

input.content:
ATN — Ops Polish

Operational robustness + visibility improvements that stack cleanly:
(a) shell-escape fix for coordinator commands, (b) terminal snapshot
tooling so diagnostic captures don't require a replay crate invocation,
and (c) per-agent watchdog for stall/liveness detection.

## Steps

1. priority-escape — fix the `(priority: High)` shell syntax error.
   Canned-command formatting bakes raw values into shell-interpreted
   strings; parens, `<`, `>`, `$`, `"`, `'`, `\`, spaces can all break
   the PTY write. Audit every `coordinator_command` / canned-action
   emitter (CannedAction, any `format!` that builds a shell line),
   introduce a `shell_escape` helper (prefer single-quote-then-insert
   style), add table-driven unit tests for the escapers. Fixes the
   known-issue in docs/status.md.

2. pty-screenshot-core — `vte`-driven terminal snapshot. New module
   (likely under `atn-pty` as `snapshot.rs`, or a thin shared helper
   used by both atn-server and atn-replay) that takes the last-N
   transcript bytes (or a live stream tap) and renders the current
   terminal grid as plain text / ansi / html. Factor out anything
   that's already in atn-replay to avoid duplication.

3. pty-screenshot-endpoint — `GET /api/agents/{id}/screenshot?format=text|ansi|html&rows=40&cols=120`,
   backed by the current transcript. Returns text/plain by default.
   Stable shape so atn-cli (next saga) can wrap it. Integration test
   against the 3-agent demo fixture.

4. pty-screenshot-ui — `📸` icon in each window chrome next to the
   existing action icons; click opens the snapshot in a new tab (so
   the user can copy/paste the text). Hovering the icon shows a
   tooltip with the endpoint URL. `window.snapshotAgent(id)` exposed
   for devtools.

5. watchdog-core — per-agent output-stall detection (>N seconds no
   output while state is `running`) + process-liveness check.
   Configurable per agent (`watchdog.stall_secs`,
   `watchdog.max_running_secs` in SpawnSpec). Pipes a new
   `OutputSignal::Stalled` into the state tracker so it's visible
   alongside Disconnected/Idle.

6. watchdog-actions — on stall, send Ctrl-C; on repeat-stall within
   a window, post a `blocked_notice` push event (routes to
   coordinator) and optionally restart the agent. Stalled state
   paints an amber outline on the window chrome + the sparkline-row
   cell. Dashboard shows a "stalled" badge near the state chip.

## Success metrics

- Canned commands with `(priority: X)` round-trip through a bash PTY
  intact (verified via integration test).
- `/api/agents/<id>/screenshot` returns a useful plain-text snapshot
  for any agent with ≥1 line of output.
- A manually-stalled fake-shim agent triggers Ctrl-C within
  watchdog.stall_secs and posts a `blocked_notice`.
- cargo test + clippy + doc clean.

ATN — Ops Polish

Operational robustness + visibility improvements that stack cleanly:
(a) shell-escape fix for coordinator commands, (b) terminal snapshot
tooling so diagnostic captures don't require a replay crate invocation,
and (c) per-agent watchdog for stall/liveness detection.

## Steps

1. priority-escape — fix the `(priority: High)` shell syntax error.
   Canned-command formatting bakes raw values into shell-interpreted
   strings; parens, `<`, `>`, `$`, `"`, `'`, `\`, spaces can all break
   the PTY write. Audit every `coordinator_command` / canned-action
   emitter (CannedAction, any `format!` that builds a shell line),
   introduce a `shell_escape` helper (prefer single-quote-then-insert
   style), add table-driven unit tests for the escapers. Fixes the
   known-issue in docs/status.md.

2. pty-screenshot-core — `vte`-driven terminal snapshot. New module
   (likely under `atn-pty` as `snapshot.rs`, or a thin shared helper
   used by both atn-server and atn-replay) that takes the last-N
   transcript bytes (or a live stream tap) and renders the current
   terminal grid as plain text / ansi / html. Factor out anything
   that's already in atn-replay to avoid duplication.

3. pty-screenshot-endpoint — `GET /api/agents/{id}/screenshot?format=text|ansi|html&rows=40&cols=120`,
   backed by the current transcript. Returns text/plain by default.
   Stable shape so atn-cli (next saga) can wrap it. Integration test
   against the 3-agent demo fixture.

4. pty-screenshot-ui — `📸` icon in each window chrome next to the
   existing action icons; click opens the snapshot in a new tab (so
   the user can copy/paste the text). Hovering the icon shows a
   tooltip with the endpoint URL. `window.snapshotAgent(id)` exposed
   for devtools.

5. watchdog-core — per-agent output-stall detection (>N seconds no
   output while state is `running`) + process-liveness check.
   Configurable per agent (`watchdog.stall_secs`,
   `watchdog.max_running_secs` in SpawnSpec). Pipes a new
   `OutputSignal::Stalled` into the state tracker so it's visible
   alongside Disconnected/Idle.

6. watchdog-actions — on stall, send Ctrl-C; on repeat-stall within
   a window, post a `blocked_notice` push event (routes to
   coordinator) and optionally restart the agent. Stalled state
   paints an amber outline on the window chrome + the sparkline-row
   cell. Dashboard shows a "stalled" badge near the state chip.

## Success metrics

- Canned commands with `(priority: X)` round-trip through a bash PTY
  intact (verified via integration test).
- `/api/agents/<id>/screenshot` returns a useful plain-text snapshot
  for any agent with ≥1 line of output.
- A manually-stalled fake-shim agent triggers Ctrl-C within
  watchdog.stall_secs and posts a `blocked_notice`.
- cargo test + clippy + doc clean.

## Step 1: Shell-escape fix for coordinator commands

Fix the known issue where `(priority: High)` in a coordinator-generated
command breaks bash because parentheses aren't escaped. Audit every
site that composes a shell line from user/agent-sourced data and
funnel through a shared escape helper.

### Deliverables

1. New `shell_escape` helper in `atn-core` (or `atn-pty/src/writer.rs` if
   that's a tighter fit). Single-quote strategy: wrap the whole arg in
   single quotes; replace interior `'` with `'\''`. Covers parens,
   spaces, `<`, `>`, `$`, `"`, `;`, `&`, `|`, `\`, backticks, newlines.
2. Audit callers:
   - `atn-pty/src/writer.rs` `input_event_to_bytes` — `CoordinatorCommand`
     writes `{command}\r`. Callers that construct that command must
     quote any injected segment.
   - `canned_action_to_bytes` — `ClaudeGo`, `ReadWiki(page)`,
     `Ack(request_id)`. `page` and `request_id` come from external
     data and currently land unescaped.
   - atn-server push-event injector: anywhere a `CoordinatorCommand`
     is built from PushEvent fields (summary, priority, target).
3. Table-driven unit tests in `atn-core` for `shell_escape`:
   empty string, no specials, single quotes inside, mixed specials,
   unicode.
4. Integration test in `atn-pty/tests` that writes a command
   containing `(priority: High)` and shell metacharacters via
   `CoordinatorCommand`, runs it through a real bash PTY, and asserts
   that bash neither errors nor parses parens as a subshell.

### Acceptance

- `docs/status.md` known-issue bullet removed (the bash parens bug).
- cargo test, clippy, doc all clean.
- No regressions in the three-agent integration test.
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -5

## Step 2: vte-driven terminal snapshot core

Add a shared snapshot helper that turns the last-N transcript bytes
into a rendered terminal grid (text, ANSI, or HTML). atn-replay
already has vt100-based rendering for full-session replay; this
shares that implementation so both the CLI and the HTTP endpoint
produce identical output.

### Deliverables

1. New module — either `atn-pty::snapshot` or a thin `atn-snapshot`
   helper crate — exposing:
   ```rust
   pub struct TerminalSnapshot { pub rows: usize, pub cols: usize,
                                  pub cells: Vec<CellRow> }
   pub fn snapshot_from_bytes(bytes: &[u8], rows: usize, cols: usize)
       -> TerminalSnapshot;
   pub fn render_text(&self) -> String;
   pub fn render_ansi(&self) -> String;   // preserves fg/bg/bold
   pub fn render_html(&self) -> String;   // minimal CSS, self-contained
   ```
2. Factor atn-replay's rendering over the same core if they diverge,
   so there's one truth for "what does the terminal look like right
   now".
3. Unit tests: simple escape sequences (cursor move, color, clear
   screen) all produce the expected grid.
4. Benchmarks live in criterion only if needed; simple asserts are
   fine for the unit tests here.

### Acceptance

- `cargo test -p atn-pty` (or whichever crate owns the module) passes
  snapshot round-trip tests.
- atn-replay still renders transcripts correctly (no regression).
- cargo doc warning-free.
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -5

## Step 3: /api/agents/{id}/screenshot HTTP endpoint

Expose the terminal snapshot over HTTP so the UI and atn-cli can
fetch a rendered snapshot without reaching into transcript files.

   - `format=text|ansi|html` (default `text`)
   - `rows=N` (default `40`)
   - `cols=N` (default `120`)
2. Backing source: read the last ~rows*cols*8 bytes from the active
   transcript (`{log_dir}/{agent_id}/transcript.log`) or, if present,
   subscribe briefly to the live broadcast. Tail-reading is fine for
   step 3; live-tap is optional.
3. Content-Type follows `format`: `text/plain; charset=utf-8`,
   `text/plain; charset=utf-8` (ansi — document that it's raw), or
   `text/html; charset=utf-8`.
4. Integration test in `crates/atn-server/tests/` that spawns the
   three-agent demo topology, types known input into one agent, and
   asserts the screenshot text includes the expected line.

### Acceptance

- curl -s /api/agents/<id>/screenshot returns useful text for every
  demo agent.
- Bad format → 400 with a short error.
- Unknown id → 404.
- cargo test + clippy clean.
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -5

input.command:
agentrail add --slug watchdog-core --task-type rust-pty-integration --prompt "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Step 5: Per-agent output-stall watchdog

Detect agents that have gone quiet while nominally `running` and
surface it as a first-class signal. Foundation for step 6's auto-
Ctrl-C + blocked_notice path.

### Deliverables

1. New `Watchdog` struct in `atn-pty` (or `atn-core` if it fits pure
   domain types) tracking:
   - `last_output_at: Instant` per agent
   - `stall_secs: u64` (default 60)
   - `max_running_secs: u64` (default 600, optional)
2. SpawnSpec gains optional `[watchdog]` fields:
   `stall_secs`, `max_running_secs`. Defaults apply when absent.
3. New `OutputSignal::Stalled` variant (or equivalent on the state
   tracker) fires when `Instant::now() - last_output_at > stall_secs`
   AND state is `running`. Clears when output resumes.
4. State tracker owns the watchdog timer; spawn-time wiring in
   atn-server rehydrates the watchdog from the spec.
5. Unit tests in atn-pty: simulate bursts of output + quiet gaps,
   assert Stalled fires exactly once per stall event.

### Acceptance

- `GET /api/agents/<id>/state` reports `{state: 'running', stalled:
  true, stalled_for_secs: N}` when a fake-shim is blocked.
- cargo test + clippy clean.
- No false positives for agents legitimately `idle` or `awaiting_human_input`.
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -5

input.command:
agentrail add --slug watchdog-actions --task-type rust-pty-integration --prompt "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Step 6: Watchdog actions — Ctrl-C, blocked_notice, UI badge

Turn the stall signal from step 5 into real consequences: nudge the
agent, post a blocked_notice for the coordinator, and paint the UI.

### Deliverables

1. Watchdog policy in atn-server:
   - First stall event: send Ctrl-C (canned action) to the agent's
     PTY. Log `watchdog.ctrl_c` event.
   - Repeat stall within 2 * stall_secs: post a `blocked_notice`
     PushEvent with `summary=\"agent stalled for Ns; watchdog sent
     Ctrl-C\"`. Route normally (should land on coordinator's inbox).
   - Optional third strike (configurable): restart.
2. `max_running_secs` path (separate from stall): if an agent is
   running uninterrupted for that long, escalate with a
   `blocked_notice`. No auto-restart by default.
3. UI: stalled windows paint an amber outline on their chrome +
   sparkline-row cell (`.stalled` class). Clears when state changes.
4. Add a `docs/windowed-ui.md` paragraph covering the watchdog
   affordance + how to tune `watchdog.stall_secs` per agent.
5. `docs/status.md` — remove the known-issue bullet about state
   tracking fragility once this ships.

### Acceptance

- Manually stall a fake-shim: watchdog sends Ctrl-C within
  stall_secs; second stall posts a blocked_notice to the
  coordinator inbox (confirm via `/api/events`).
- Dashboard shows amber outline on the stalled window.
- cargo test + clippy + doc clean.
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -5

content.content:
1	use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
2	
3	/// Input sent to an agent's PTY.
4	#[derive(Clone, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
5	#[serde(tag = "type", rename_all = "snake_case")]
6	pub enum InputEvent {
7	    HumanText { text: String },
8	    RawBytes { bytes: Vec<u8> },
9	    CoordinatorCommand { command: String },
10	    Action { action: CannedAction },
11	}
12	
13	/// Pre-defined actions with known byte sequences or workflows.
14	#[derive(Clone, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq)]
15	#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
16	pub enum CannedAction {
17	    CtrlC,
18	    ClaudeGo,
19	    ReadWiki { page: String },
20	    Ack { request_id: String },
21	}
22	
23	/// Signal emitted from the PTY output parser.
24	#[derive(Clone, Debug)]
25	pub enum OutputSignal {
26	    Bytes(Vec<u8>),
27	    PromptReady,
28	    QuestionDetected { snippet: String },
29	    IdleDetected,
30	    PushEvent(PushEvent),
31	    /// Reader hit EOF or an error on the PTY master. The child has exited or
32	    /// the transport dropped; the agent should transition to Disconnected and
33	    /// the UI should offer a reconnect affordance.
34	    Disconnected,
35	}
36	
37	/// A structured inter-agent push event.
38	#[derive(Clone, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
39	pub struct PushEvent {
40	    pub id: String,
41	    pub kind: PushKind,
42	    pub source_agent: String,
43	    pub source_repo: String,
44	    pub target_agent: Option<String>,
45	    pub issue_id: Option<String>,
46	    pub summary: String,
47	    pub wiki_link: Option<String>,
48	    pub priority: Priority,
49	    pub timestamp: String,
50	}
51	
52	/// Type of inter-agent push event.
53	#[derive(Clone, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq)]
54	#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
55	pub enum PushKind {
56	    FeatureRequest,
57	    BugFixRequest,
58	    CompletionNotice,
59	    BlockedNotice,
60	    NeedsInfo,
61	    VerificationRequest,
62	}
63	
64	/// Priority level for a push event.
65	#[derive(Clone, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq)]
66	#[serde(rename_all = "lowercase")]
67	pub enum Priority {
68	    Normal,
69	    High,
70	    Blocking,
71	}
72	
73	#[cfg(test)]
74	mod tests {
75	    use super::*;
76	
77	    #[test]
78	    fn push_event_roundtrip() {
79	        let event = PushEvent {
80	            id: "evt-001".to_string(),
81	            kind: PushKind::FeatureRequest,
82	            source_agent: "frontend-dev".to_string(),
83	            source_repo: "frontend".to_string(),
84	            target_agent: Some("backend-dev".to_string()),
85	            issue_id: Some("FEAT-42".to_string()),
86	            summary: "Need API endpoint for user profiles".to_string(),
87	            wiki_link: Some("Coordination/Requests".to_string()),
88	            priority: Priority::High,
89	            timestamp: "2026-03-29T14:00:00Z".to_string(),

## Known Issues

- ATN notification `(priority: High)` causes bash syntax error (unquoted parentheses)
- State tracking timing is fragile for programmatic task completion detection

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# All Together Now — Status

## Current State: Windowed-UI Saga Complete

The dashboard now ships a desktop-window-manager model (Tiled / Stack
/ Carousel + per-window chrome + keyboard Option C). The heat-sized
treemap (scale-UI saga) is preserved as the legacy model for
large-fleet use — see
[docs/windowed-ui.md](./windowed-ui.md) for the new model and
[docs/scale-ui.md](./scale-ui.md) for the legacy walkthrough.

## Prior Milestone: Remote-Agent Demo Saga Complete

Phases 0–8 shipped plus the follow-on **remote-agent demo saga** (5 steps,
all shipped in this sequence):

1. **empty-start** — ATN boots with zero agents; UI renders empty state
   with a + New Agent CTA. `agents.toml` defaults to `[project]`-only; seed
   preserved as `agents.example.toml`.
2. **new-agent-dialog** — structured `SpawnSpec` (name/role/transport/
   host/user/working_dir/project/agent/agent_args) in `atn-core`; `POST
   /api/agents` validates and composes shell commands for local/mosh/ssh.
   Both the Yew modal and the static HTML form use the same schema.
3. **remote-pty-transport** — `OutputSignal::Disconnected` signal pipes
   PTY EOF → `AgentState::Disconnected`; `POST /api/agents/{id}/reconnect`
   re-attaches to remote tmux; graceful delete sends `^B :kill-session` to
   clean up tmux server-side. `tools/fake-mosh` + symlinks back the
   integration tests.
4. **three-agent-demo** — `tools/fake-claude`, `tools/fake-codex`,
   `tools/fake-opencode-glm5` shims; `demos/three-agent/fixtures/*.json`
   + `setup.sh` (`ATN_DEMO_REAL=1` for real CLIs); `ATN_PORT` env +
   `atn-server ready on <addr>` stdout marker; `crates/atn-server/tests/
   three_agent_demo.rs` end-to-end integration test.
5. **docs-refresh** — this doc + `docs/usage.md`, `docs/demo-three-agent.md`,
   `docs/uber-use-case.md` cross-links, README quickstart.

End-to-end multi-agent demo working with reg-rs regression test.

## What Exists

| Crate | Status | Description |
|-------|--------|-------------|
| `atn-core` | Complete | Domain types: agent, event, inbox, error, config, router |
| `atn-pty` | Complete | PTY session management, reader, writer (with input logging), state tracker, transcript |
| `atn-server` | Complete | Axum HTTP/SSE server with all REST endpoints + agent CRUD |
| `atn-wiki` | Complete | FileWikiStorage + coordination page seeding |
| `atn-trail` | Complete | Agentrail file reader + CLI wrapper |
| `atn-replay` | Complete | PTY transcript viewer: screenshot, dashboard (org-mode), HTML output |
| `atn-ui` | Placeholder | Yew frontend (server uses embedded static HTML) |

## Phase Summary

| Phase | Description | Status |
|-------|-------------|--------|
| 0 | Project skeleton, workspace, core types | Done |
| 1 | Single agent PTY with integration tests | Done |
| 2 | Minimal web UI with xterm.js + SSE | Done |
| 3 | Multi-agent management with dashboard grid | Done |
| 4 | Wiki integration with REST API + CAS | Done |
| 5 | Message routing with outbox polling | Done |
| 6 | Agentrail integration with saga UI | Done |
| 7 | Polish: shutdown, restart, graph, notifications, hot-reload | Done |
| 8 | Session management UI (agent CRUD from browser) | Done |
| — | Demo, input logging, atn-replay crate | Done |
| R1 | Empty-start: boot with zero agents | Done |
| R2 | New Agent dialog: structured SpawnSpec + compose | Done |
| R3 | Remote PTY transport: reconnect + tmux cleanup | Done |
| R4 | Three-agent demo: fake shims + integration test | Done |
| R5 | Docs refresh: usage, demo walkthrough, cross-links | Done |
| S1..S5 | Scale-UI saga: heat score, compact tile, CSS-scaled xterm, treemap, pin + keyboard | Done |
| S6 | Demo scripts: docs/demos-scripts.md menu of runnable demos | Done |
| S7 | Search + chips + group-by + saved layouts | Done |
| S8 | Scale-UI fleet (21 fake agents) + docs/scale-ui.md walkthrough | Done |
| W1 | Windowed-UI tiled foundation: strip treemap/squarify; coord-left + workers grid | Done |
| W2 | Window chrome: per-panel min/max/pin/config/reconnect/delete + click-to-select + bottom dock | Done |
| W3 | Stack layout: one primary at ~80% + dock of minimized; enter/leave reconcile state | Done |
| W4 | Carousel layout: primary + prev/next peeks + `◀/▶` cycle (excludes minimized) | Done |
| W5 | Keyboard Option C: `m`/`M`/`p`/`←→`/`1..9`/`Esc` on selected, guarded by xterm focus | Done |
| W6 | Persistence (`atn-window-ui-v1`) + sort selector + `docs/windowed-ui.md` + `demos/windowed-ui/setup.sh` + Demo 9 + scale-ui.md legacy banner | Done |

## Demo

Two AI agents collaborate via ATN to build a CLI app:
1. Dev agent creates `app.py` with a `greet` command (via opencode + deepseek)
2. ATN routes a feature request to feature agent
3. Feature agent reads `app.py` and adds a `farewell` command
4. Both commands verified working; completion notice routed back

Run: `bash demo/run-demo.sh` (~55 seconds, ~$0.006 API cost)
Capture artifacts: `ATN_CAPTURE_DIR=demo/last-run bash demo/run-demo.sh`
Regression test: `REG_RS_DATA_DIR=work/reg-rs reg-rs run -p atn-demo`

## Replay Tooling

`atn-replay` renders PTY transcripts from `.atn/logs/*/transcript.log`:

```
atn-replay dashboard .atn/logs -o dashboard.org   # emacs auto-revert
atn-replay screenshot transcript.log              # text box to stdout
atn-replay screenshot transcript.log --html f.html # standalone HTML
atn-replay screenshot transcript.log --html-fragment f.html  # org embed
atn-replay list .atn/logs                         # list agents + sizes
```

## Logging

Three log files per agent in `.atn/logs/{agent_id}/`:
- `transcript.log` — raw PTY output bytes (replayable via atn-replay)
- `inputs.jsonl` — timestamped input commands (human_text, coordinator_command, etc.)
- `events.jsonl` — state transitions (prompt_ready, idle_detected, push_event)

## Architecture

Cargo workspace with 7 crates (library-first design):
- **atn-core**: pure domain types, no async, no I/O
- **atn-pty**: PTY session lifecycle via portable-pty + tokio
- **atn-server**: Axum binary with REST + SSE + embedded static UI
- **atn-wiki**: wiki storage and coordination logic
- **atn-trail**: agentrail file reader and CLI wrapper
- **atn-replay**: PTY transcript rendering (vt100 + clap)
- **atn-ui**: Yew components (placeholder)

## Known Issues

- ATN notification `(priority: High)` causes bash syntax error (unquoted parentheses)
- State tracking timing is fragile for programmatic task completion detection

## Upstream Projects

### agentrail-rs
**Location**: `~/github/sw-vibe-coding/agentrail-rs`
**Integration**: CLI binary in PATH, invoked by `atn-trail/src/cli.rs`

### wiki-rs
**Location**: `~/github/sw-vibe-coding/wiki-rs`
**Integration**: `wiki-common` crate used as path dependency (with `server` feature)

## Quality

- Zero clippy warnings (`cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings`)
- All tests passing (`cargo test --workspace`)
- Rust 2024 edition throughout

input.new_string:
# Changes

- Ops-polish saga step 1 (shell-escape for coordinator commands): fixes the `(priority: High)` bash-syntax-error known-issue in `docs/status.md`. New `atn_core::shell::shell_escape` helper wraps a string in single quotes and escapes interior single quotes with the classic `'\''` dance — the result is always a single POSIX shell word even when the input has parens, spaces, `<`, `>`, `$`, `|`, `&`, `;`, `"`, `'`, `\`, backticks, newlines, or unicode. `canned_action_to_bytes` in `atn-pty::writer` now pipes `CannedAction::ReadWiki.page` and `CannedAction::Ack.request_id` through the helper before formatting the `coord read …\n` / `coord ack …\n` lines that hit the PTY. `CoordinatorCommand { command }` and `setup_commands` are left unescaped by design (they're full shell snippets); `HumanText` stays as-is (LLM agents interpret text, not shell). 10 table-driven unit tests in `atn-core` cover the escape helper (empty string, interior quotes, parens+colon, metacharacter passthrough, unicode, the always-single-word invariant). New `atn-pty` integration tests drive a real bash PTY through `CannedAction::ReadWiki { page: "Requests (priority: High)" }` and `CannedAction::Ack { request_id: "REQ-can't-touch-this" }` and assert the output carries no `syntax error` / `unexpected EOF` and does carry `coord: command not found` — i.e. bash parsed the line as a single word. Ops-polish saga begins; the known-issue bullet for this bug is removed from `docs/status.md`. cargo test (workspace), clippy, doc all clean.
- Windowed-UI saga step 6 (persistence, docs, demo):

# Changes

- Ops-polish saga step 1 (shell-escape for coordinator commands): fixes the `(priority: High)` bash-syntax-error known-issue in `docs/status.md`. New `atn_core::shell::shell_escape` helper wraps a string in single quotes and escapes interior single quotes with the classic `'\''` dance — the result is always a single POSIX shell word even when the input has parens, spaces, `<`, `>`, `$`, `|`, `&`, `;`, `"`, `'`, `\`, backticks, newlines, or unicode. `canned_action_to_bytes` in `atn-pty::writer` now pipes `CannedAction::ReadWiki.page` and `CannedAction::Ack.request_id` through the helper before formatting the `coord read …\n` / `coord ack …\n` lines that hit the PTY. `CoordinatorCommand { command }` and `setup_commands` are left unescaped by design (they're full shell snippets); `HumanText` stays as-is (LLM agents interpret text, not shell). 10 table-driven unit tests in `atn-core` cover the escape helper (empty string, interior quotes, parens+colon, metacharacter passthrough, unicode, the always-single-word invariant). lines: +- Ops-polish saga step 1 (shell-escape for coordinator commands): fixes the `(priority: High)` bash-syntax-error known-issue in `docs/status.md`. New `atn_core::shell::shell_escape` helper wraps a string in single quotes and escapes interior single quotes with the classic `'\''` dance — the result is always a single POSIX shell word even when the input has parens, spaces, `<`, `>`, `$`, `|`, `&`, `;`, `"`, `'`, `\`, backticks, newlines, or unicode. `canned_action_to_bytes` in `atn-pty::writer` now pipes `CannedAction::ReadWiki.page` and `CannedAction::Ack.request_id` through the helper before formatting the `coord read …\n` / `coord ack …\n` lines that hit the PTY. `CoordinatorCommand { command }` and `setup_commands` are left unescaped by design (they're full shell snippets); `HumanText` stays as-is (LLM agents interpret text, not shell). 10 table-driven unit tests in `atn-core` cover the escape helper (empty string, interior quotes, parens+colon, metacharacter passthrough, unicode, the always-single-word invariant). New `atn-pty` integration tests drive a real bash PTY through `CannedAction::ReadWiki { page: "Requests (priority: High)" }` and `CannedAction::Ack { request_id: "REQ-can't-touch-this" }` and assert the output carries no `syntax error` / `unexpected EOF` and does carry `coord: command not found` — i.e. bash parsed the line as a single word. Ops-polish saga begins; the known-issue bullet for this bug is removed from `docs/status.md`. cargo test (workspace), clippy, doc all clean.

- Windowed-UI saga step 6 (persistence, docs, demo): closes the saga. New `localStorage` key **`atn-window-ui-v1`** carries just the windowed-UI state — `layoutMode`, `sortMode`, `selectedId`, and per-agent `windows[id] = { state, pinned, pinnedRect }` (omitting default-state windows to keep the blob small). `saveWindowUiPrefs` / `loadWindowUiPrefs` handle it; `saveLayoutPrefs` now fans out to both keys and `windowSelect` + `setSortMode` trigger saves so selection + sort choice survive a hard refresh. New top-bar **sort selector** (`Name` default / `Recent` = smoothed-heat-desc) is wired into `buildLayoutItems` so Tiled worker order + Carousel ring follow the choice. New `demos/windowed-ui/setup.sh` (1 coord + 3 workers, local-fake-shim default, `ATN_DEMO_REAL=1` opt-in) stands up the Demo 9 topology and prints a five-step pointer at layouts / chrome / keyboard / sort / persistence. New `docs/windowed-ui.md` walkthrough covers TL;DR, three layouts, chrome + pin semantics, top bar, Option-C keyboard, sparkline row, `atn-window-ui-v1` schema. `docs/demos-scripts.md` gains **Demo 9 "Windowed UI"** in the index + a full section; Demos 6 and 8 get `(legacy model)` banners pointing at Demo 9. `docs/scale-ui.md` banner at the top notes it's the legacy walkthrough and links to `windowed-ui.md`. `docs/usage.md` Dashboard section rewritten around windowed UI; links `docs/windowed-ui.md` first in the See-also list. `docs/status.md` rolls forward: **Current State** now reads "Windowed-UI Saga Complete" and W1..W6 rows (tiled-foundation, window-chrome, stack-layout, carousel-layout, keyboard-option-C, persistence-docs-demo) join S1..S8. Live smoke: 4-agent demo, state changes (stack + coord-pinned-at-rect + sort=recent) round-trip cleanly through a hard refresh; pinned rect `{143, 40, 1113, 645}` restored exactly, UI reflects pinned class + sparkline amber outline + dock population. `cargo doc --workspace --no-deps` warning-free; cargo test + clippy clean.

- Windowed-UI saga step 5 (Option-C keyboard): bare-key window-manager bindings that fire only when focus isn't in an input/textarea/xterm. Replaced the legacy scale-UI keyboard handler (1..9 hottest-unpinned, 0 coord, f focus-size, p pin-focused, `/`, Esc clear-focus) with the selected-window model: `m` minimize selected, `M` (Shift-m) maximize/restore toggle, `p` pin/unpin, `←/→` cycle focus prev/next by sort order (excluding minimized), `1..9` jump to Nth in sort order, `/` focuses the filter input, `Esc` restores any maximized window on first press then deselects on second. Extra `isXtermFocused()` guard checks `document.activeElement.closest('.xterm')` so xterm's own textarea doesn't leak bindings through. Helpers: `keyboardCycleItems`, `keyboardJump`, `keyboardCycle`. Top-bar `#kbd-hint` strip shows the bindings as muted text next to the carousel nav. Per-panel `.pty-focus-badge` ("typing to PTY", amber pill near Send) toggles via a `focusin`/`focusout` document listener that tracks whether the active element is inside an `.xterm`, adding `.pty-focused` to the owning `.agent-panel`. Live browser smoke (3-agent demo): click coord header → `m` minimizes it (moves to dock); select hlasm → `M` maximizes to 1113px (~80%); `Esc` restores, second `Esc` deselects; `2` jumps to the 2nd cycle item; `9` is a no-op with only 2 items; `←`/`→` wrap; `p` pins at the current rect; focusing the xterm textarea then pressing `m` is correctly ignored (selection unchanged, no minimize). cargo test + clippy clean.

- Windowed-UI saga step 4 (carousel layout): third layout mode with a focused primary at ~80% of viewport + prev/next peeks (~9%) on either side, plus `[◀]` / `[▶]` cycle buttons in the top bar. `computeLayout` gains a carousel branch that filters out manually-minimized agents (they stay in the bottom dock, deliverable 5) and builds a name-ordered ring: focus defaults to `selectedId` → coordinator → first, prev/next wrap around the ends, and n≤2 collapses to just the primary or primary+one peek. `setLayoutMode` now accepts `'carousel'` (leaves stack mode when coming from stack, clears any lingering maximized state so carousel/stack math isn't short-circuited). New `carouselCycle(delta)` moves `selectedId` through the eligible ring; `.carousel-btn[data-carousel]` buttons wire prev/next. `windowSelect` re-runs the layout when carousel is active so clicking a header snaps that window to the primary slot. Compact-tile `swap-to-focus` handler also updates `selectedId` so clicking a peek (rendered compact at 9% width) promotes it. Dock-cell clicks in carousel restore + focus. `#carousel-nav` toggles via `updateCarouselNav`. Live browser smoke (3-agent demo): Tiled → Carousel lays out coord primary (1126px of 1400), worker-rpg left peek (125px), worker-hlasm right peek (125px); `◀/▶` cycle shuffles name-order ring; peek click jumps to center; manually minimized worker-hlasm goes to dock and is skipped by cycle; dock-click restores + focuses; Tiled toggle back restores 3-tile grid; persistence round-trips. cargo test + clippy clean.

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(pty): shell-escape CannedAction page + request_id (fixes parens bug)

Step 1 of the ops-polish saga. Resolves the docs/status.md known
issue: `(priority: High)` in a coordinator-generated `coord read …` /
`coord ack …` line broke bash with a "syntax error near unexpected
token `('" because the parens weren't quoted.

- New `atn_core::shell::shell_escape` wraps arbitrary text in single
  quotes, using the classic `'\''` dance for interior single quotes.
  Covers parens, whitespace, `<`, `>`, `$`, `|`, `&`, `;`, `"`, `\`,
  backticks, newlines, and unicode (single-quoted strings preserve
  everything).
- `atn-pty::writer::canned_action_to_bytes` now threads ReadWiki.page
  and Ack.request_id through the helper before formatting the shell
  line. CoordinatorCommand + setup_commands remain unescaped by
  design (they're full shell snippets). HumanText stays as-is
  (targets LLM agents, not shells).
- 10 table-driven unit tests in atn-core cover the helper; two new
  atn-pty integration tests drive a real bash PTY through
  CannedAction::ReadWiki { page: "Requests (priority: High)" } and
  CannedAction::Ack { request_id: "REQ-can't-touch-this" } and
  assert no syntax error / unexpected EOF in the output.

cargo test + clippy + doc all clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

Description: ## Step 2: vte-driven terminal snapshot core

Add a shared snapshot helper th...
Task type: rust-workspace-setup

=== PROMPT ===
## Step 2: vte-driven terminal snapshot core

Add a shared snapshot helper that turns the last-N transcript bytes
into a rendered terminal grid (text, ANSI, or HTML). atn-replay
already has vt100-based rendering for full-session replay; this
shares that implementation so both the CLI and the HTTP endpoint
produce identical output.

### Deliverables

1. New module — either `atn-pty::snapshot` or a thin `atn-snapshot`
   helper crate — exposing:
   ```rust
   pub struct TerminalSnapshot { pub rows: usize, pub cols: usize,
                                  pub cells: Vec<CellRow> }
   pub fn snapshot_from_bytes(bytes: &[u8], rows: usize, cols: usize)
       -> TerminalSnapshot;
   pub fn render_text(&self) -> String;
   pub fn render_ansi(&self) -> String;   // preserves fg/bg/bold
   pub fn render_html(&self) -> String;   // minimal CSS, self-contained
   ```
2. Factor atn-replay's rendering over the same core if they diverge,
   so there's one truth for "what does the terminal look like right
   now".
3. Unit tests: simple escape sequences (cursor move, color, clear
   screen) all produce the expected grid.
4. Benchmarks live in criterion only if needed; simple asserts are
   fine for the unit tests here.

### Acceptance

- `cargo test -p atn-pty` (or whichever crate owns the module) passes
  snapshot round-trip tests.
- atn-replay still renders transcripts correctly (no regression).
- cargo doc warning-free.

=== WHEN DONE ===
Run: agentrail begin    (if not yet started)
Run: agentrail complete --summary "what you did" --next-slug <slug> --next-prompt "next instructions"
  Or: agentrail complete --summary "what you did" --done   (if this is the last step)

already has vt100-based rendering for full-session replay; this
shares that implementation so both the CLI and the HTTP endpoint
produce identical output.

### Deliverables

1. New module — either `atn-pty::snapshot` or a thin `atn-snapshot`
   helper crate — exposing:
   ```rust
   pub struct TerminalSnapshot { pub rows: usize, pub cols: usize,
                                  pub cells: Vec<CellRow> }
   pub fn snapshot_from_bytes(bytes: &[u8], rows: usize, cols: usize)
       -> TerminalSnapshot;
   pub fn render_text(&self) -> String;
   pub fn render_ansi(&self) -> String;   // preserves fg/bg/bold
   pub fn render_html(&self) -> String;   // minimal CSS, self-contained
   ```
2. Factor atn-replay's rendering over the same core if they diverge,
   so there's one truth for "what does the terminal look like right
   now".
3. Unit tests: simple escape sequences (cursor move, color, clear
   screen) all produce the expected grid.
4. Benchmarks live in criterion only if needed; simple asserts are
   fine for the unit tests here.

### Acceptance

- `cargo test -p atn-pty` (or whichever crate owns the module) passes
  snapshot round-trip tests.
- atn-replay still renders transcripts correctly (no regression).
- cargo doc warning-free.

=== WHEN DONE ===
Run: agentrail begin    (if not yet started)
Run: agentrail complete --summary "what you did" --next-slug <slug> --next-prompt "next instructions"
  Or: agentrail complete --summary "what you did" --done   (if this is the last step)

input.new_string:
# Changes

- Ops-polish saga step 2 (vt100-driven terminal snapshot core): new `atn_pty::snapshot` module that parses any PTY-output byte stream through a `vt100::Parser` at a given rows × cols geometry and produces a `TerminalSnapshot { rows, cols, cells: Vec<CellRow> }` with one `Cell { ch, fg, bg, bold, italic, underline, inverse }` per grid position. Three rendering targets share one source of truth: `render_text()` strips style, `render_ansi()` emits SGR escapes with per-row resets so paste-into-terminal behaves, and `render_html()` emits a self-contained `<pre>` with inline-styled `<span>` runs (coalesces same-style cells, maps the standard 16-colour + 6×6×6 cube + 24-step greyscale palette to CSS `rgb` hexes). Geometry is clamped to ≥ 1×1 to dodge vt100's zero-size panic. `vt100 = "0.15"` hoisted into the workspace dependency table so `atn-pty` and `atn-replay` both pull from the same pin. 11 unit tests cover cursor-move + overwrite, clear-screen, ANSI colour / bold round-trips, HTML escaping, zero-geometry clamp, empty-input grid, palette spot-checks, and the `same_style` cell coalescer. `atn-replay` left functionally unchanged (runs through its own `vt100` usage) — step 3 will cut the HTTP endpoint over to the new core; factoring `atn-replay` onto it is a follow-up if output ever diverges. cargo test + clippy + doc warning-free.
- Ops-polish saga step 1 (shell-escape for coordinator commands):

- Ops-polish saga step 2 (vt100-driven terminal snapshot core): new `atn_pty::snapshot` module that parses any PTY-output byte stream through a `vt100::Parser` at a given rows × cols geometry and produces a `TerminalSnapshot { rows, cols, cells: Vec<CellRow> }` with one `Cell { ch, fg, bg, bold, italic, underline, inverse }` per grid position. lines: +- Ops-polish saga step 2 (vt100-driven terminal snapshot core): new `atn_pty::snapshot` module that parses any PTY-output byte stream through a `vt100::Parser` at a given rows × cols geometry and produces a `TerminalSnapshot { rows, cols, cells: Vec<CellRow> }` with one `Cell { ch, fg, bg, bold, italic, underline, inverse }` per grid position. Three rendering targets share one source of truth: `render_text()` strips style, `render_ansi()` emits SGR escapes with per-row resets so paste-into-terminal behaves, and `render_html()` emits a self-contained `<pre>` with inline-styled `<span>` runs (coalesces same-style cells, maps the standard 16-colour + 6×6×6 cube + 24-step greyscale palette to CSS `rgb` hexes). Geometry is clamped to ≥ 1×1 to dodge vt100's zero-size panic. `vt100 = "0.15"` hoisted into the workspace dependency table so `atn-pty` and `atn-replay` both pull from the same pin. 11 unit tests cover cursor-move + overwrite, clear-screen, ANSI colour / bold round-trips, HTML escaping, zero-geometry clamp, empty-input grid, palette spot-checks, and the `same_style` cell coalescer. `atn-replay` left functionally unchanged (runs through its own `vt100` usage) — step 3 will cut the HTTP endpoint over to the new core; factoring `atn-replay` onto it is a follow-up if output ever diverges. cargo test + clippy + doc warning-free.

- Ops-polish saga step 1 (shell-escape for coordinator commands): fixes the `(priority: High)` bash-syntax-error known-issue in `docs/status.md`. New `atn_core::shell::shell_escape` helper wraps a string in single quotes and escapes interior single quotes with the classic `'\''` dance — the result is always a single POSIX shell word even when the input has parens, spaces, `<`, `>`, `$`, `|`, `&`, `;`, `"`, `'`, `\`, backticks, newlines, or unicode. `canned_action_to_bytes` in `atn-pty::writer` now pipes `CannedAction::ReadWiki.page` and `CannedAction::Ack.request_id` through the helper before formatting the `coord read …\n` / `coord ack …\n` lines that hit the PTY. `CoordinatorCommand { command }` and `setup_commands` are left unescaped by design (they're full shell snippets); `HumanText` stays as-is (LLM agents interpret text, not shell). 10 table-driven unit tests in `atn-core` cover the escape helper (empty string, interior quotes, parens+colon, metacharacter passthrough, unicode, the always-single-word invariant). New `atn-pty` integration tests drive a real bash PTY through `CannedAction::ReadWiki { page: "Requests (priority: High)" }` and `CannedAction::Ack { request_id: "REQ-can't-touch-this" }` and assert the output carries no `syntax error` / `unexpected EOF` and does carry `coord: command not found` — i.e. bash parsed the line as a single word. Ops-polish saga begins; the known-issue bullet for this bug is removed from `docs/status.md`. cargo test (workspace), clippy, doc all clean.

- Windowed-UI saga step 6 (persistence, docs, demo): closes the saga. New `localStorage` key **`atn-window-ui-v1`** carries just the windowed-UI state — `layoutMode`, `sortMode`, `selectedId`, and per-agent `windows[id] = { state, pinned, pinnedRect }` (omitting default-state windows to keep the blob small). `saveWindowUiPrefs` / `loadWindowUiPrefs` handle it; `saveLayoutPrefs` now fans out to both keys and `windowSelect` + `setSortMode` trigger saves so selection + sort choice survive a hard refresh. New top-bar **sort selector** (`Name` default / `Recent` = smoothed-heat-desc) is wired into `buildLayoutItems` so Tiled worker order + Carousel ring follow the choice. New `demos/windowed-ui/setup.sh` (1 coord + 3 workers, local-fake-shim default, `ATN_DEMO_REAL=1` opt-in) stands up the Demo 9 topology and prints a five-step pointer at layouts / chrome / keyboard / sort / persistence. New `docs/windowed-ui.md` walkthrough covers TL;DR, three layouts, chrome + pin semantics, top bar, Option-C keyboard, sparkline row, `atn-window-ui-v1` schema. `docs/demos-scripts.md` gains **Demo 9 "Windowed UI"** in the index + a full section; Demos 6 and 8 get `(legacy model)` banners pointing at Demo 9. `docs/scale-ui.md` banner at the top notes it's the legacy walkthrough and links to `windowed-ui.md`. `docs/usage.md` Dashboard section rewritten around windowed UI; links `docs/windowed-ui.md` first in the See-also list. `docs/status.md` rolls forward: **Current State** now reads "Windowed-UI Saga Complete" and W1..W6 rows (tiled-foundation, window-chrome, stack-layout, carousel-layout, keyboard-option-C, persistence-docs-demo) join S1..S8. Live smoke: 4-agent demo, state changes (stack + coord-pinned-at-rect + sort=recent) round-trip cleanly through a hard refresh; pinned rect `{143, 40, 1113, 645}` restored exactly, UI reflects pinned class + sparkline amber outline + dock population. `cargo doc --workspace --no-deps` warning-free; cargo test + clippy clean.

- Windowed-UI saga step 5 (Option-C keyboard): bare-key window-manager bindings that fire only when focus isn't in an input/textarea/xterm. Replaced the legacy scale-UI keyboard handler (1..9 hottest-unpinned, 0 coord, f focus-size, p pin-focused, `/`, Esc clear-focus) with the selected-window model: `m` minimize selected, `M` (Shift-m) maximize/restore toggle, `p` pin/unpin, `←/→` cycle focus prev/next by sort order (excluding minimized), `1..9` jump to Nth in sort order, `/` focuses the filter input, `Esc` restores any maximized window on first press then deselects on second. Extra `isXtermFocused()` guard checks `document.activeElement.closest('.xterm')` so xterm's own textarea doesn't leak bindings through. Helpers: `keyboardCycleItems`, `keyboardJump`, `keyboardCycle`. Top-bar `#kbd-hint` strip shows the bindings as muted text next to the carousel nav. Per-panel `.pty-focus-badge` ("typing to PTY", amber pill near Send) toggles via a `focusin`/`focusout` document listener that tracks whether the active element is inside an `.xterm`, adding `.pty-focused` to the owning `.agent-panel`. Live browser smoke (3-agent demo): click coord header → `m` minimizes it (moves to dock); select hlasm → `M` maximizes to 1113px (~80%); `Esc` restores, second `Esc` deselects; `2` jumps to the 2nd cycle item; `9` is a no-op with only 2 items; `←`/`→` wrap; `p` pins at the current rect; focusing the xterm textarea then pressing `m` is correctly ignored (selection unchanged, no minimize). cargo test + clippy clean.

git add Cargo.lock && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(pty): vt100-driven terminal snapshot core

Step 2 of the ops-polish saga. New `atn_pty::snapshot` module is the
single source of truth for "what does the terminal look like right
now". Step 3 will wrap it behind `GET /api/agents/{id}/screenshot`.

- `snapshot_from_bytes(bytes, rows, cols) -> TerminalSnapshot` parses
  any PTY byte stream through `vt100::Parser` at the given geometry
  (clamped ≥ 1×1 to dodge vt100's zero-size panic) and captures the
  resulting screen as a `Vec<CellRow>` of `Cell { ch, fg, bg, bold,
  italic, underline, inverse }`.
- Three renderers share the same cell representation:
  - `render_text()` — plain, style-stripped, trim trailing spaces.
  - `render_ansi()` — SGR escapes with per-row resets so the output
    is safe to paste into another terminal.
  - `render_html()` — self-contained `<pre>` with inline-styled
    `<span>` runs; palette index → CSS via the xterm 16-colour +
    6×6×6 cube + 24-step greyscale tables.
- `vt100 = "0.15"` hoisted into the workspace dependency table;
  atn-pty adopts it, atn-replay now references it through the
  workspace so both stay pinned.

11 unit tests cover cursor-move, clear-screen, colour + bold round-
trips, HTML escaping, zero geometry clamp, empty-input grid, palette
spot-checks, and the same-style coalescer.

content.content:
=== PROMPT ===
## Step 3: /api/agents/{id}/screenshot HTTP endpoint

Expose the terminal snapshot over HTTP so the UI and atn-cli can
fetch a rendered snapshot without reaching into transcript files.

### Deliverables

1. `GET /api/agents/{id}/screenshot` with query params:
   - `format=text|ansi|html` (default `text`)
   - `rows=N` (default `40`)
   - `cols=N` (default `120`)
2. Backing source: read the last ~rows*cols*8 bytes from the active
   transcript (`{log_dir}/{agent_id}/transcript.log`) or, if present,
   subscribe briefly to the live broadcast. Tail-reading is fine for
   step 3; live-tap is optional.
3. Content-Type follows `format`: `text/plain; charset=utf-8`,
   `text/plain; charset=utf-8` (ansi — document that it's raw), or
   `text/html; charset=utf-8`.
4. Integration test in `crates/atn-server/tests/` that spawns the
   three-agent demo topology, types known input into one agent, and
   asserts the screenshot text includes the expected line.

### Acceptance

- curl -s /api/agents/<id>/screenshot returns useful text for every
  demo agent.
- Bad format → 400 with a short error.
- Unknown id → 404.
- cargo test + clippy clean.

=== WHEN DONE ===
Run: agentrail begin    (if not yet started)
Run: agentrail complete --summary "what you did" --next-slug <slug> --next-prompt "next instructions"
  Or: agentrail complete --summary "what you did" --done   (if this is the last step)
Step 003-pty-screenshot-endpoint is now in-progress.

lines: -                // Send text, pause, then Enter as separate events.
+                // Send text + Enter in a single atomic HumanText. This
+                // avoids the pre-existing race where the event_log
+                // could be pushed after the 150ms inter-keystroke
+                // pause — tests that polled for "both inboxes written"
+                // before the second log entry landed saw only one
tracing::info!("Router: delivered event {} to agent {}", event.id, agent_id);

# Changes

input.new_string:
# Changes

- Ops-polish saga step 3 (`GET /api/agents/{id}/screenshot`): new HTTP endpoint that returns a rendered terminal snapshot. Query params `format=text|ansi|html` (default `text`), `rows=N` (default 40, clamped 1..=500), `cols=N` (default 120). `text` and `ansi` return `text/plain; charset=utf-8`; `html` returns `text/html; charset=utf-8` with the self-contained `<pre>` from `atn_pty::snapshot::render_html`. Bad format returns 400 with a short `unknown format '<x>'; expected one of text|ansi|html`; unknown agent returns 404 with `agent '<id>' not found`. Source bytes come from the tail of `{log_dir}/{id}/transcript.log` — `read_transcript_tail` seeks to `len - min(rows*cols*8, 256 KiB)` and reads forward, so the snapshot reflects recent output without pulling the whole transcript into memory. New `SessionManager::log_dir()` accessor exposes the manager's logging root to the endpoint. New integration test `crates/atn-server/tests/screenshot_endpoint.rs` boots the real binary on an ephemeral port, spawns a `fake-claude` agent, polls until the banner lands in a `text` screenshot, then exercises every response path (text default, explicit `text`, `html`, raw `ansi`, `format=bogus` → 400, unknown id → 404). Drive-by router fix: `process_outbox_file`'s DeliverToAgent branch previously sent the prompt text then slept 150ms before sending `\r`, which produced a pre-existing flake in `three_agent_topology_end_to_end` where `/api/events` could report only 1 of 2 delivered events because the second `event_log.push` hadn't run yet when the test's both-inboxes poll fired. Folded the text + `\r` into one `HumanText` so the event log is consistent with the inbox-write milestone. 5-run soak of the three-agent test now passes deterministically. cargo test + clippy + doc warning-free.
- Ops-polish saga step 2 (vt100-driven terminal snapshot core):

- Ops-polish saga step 3 (`GET /api/agents/{id}/screenshot`): new HTTP endpoint that returns a rendered terminal snapshot. lines: +- Ops-polish saga step 3 (`GET /api/agents/{id}/screenshot`): new HTTP endpoint that returns a rendered terminal snapshot. Query params `format=text|ansi|html` (default `text`), `rows=N` (default 40, clamped 1..=500), `cols=N` (default 120). `text` and `ansi` return `text/plain; charset=utf-8`; `html` returns `text/html; charset=utf-8` with the self-contained `<pre>` from `atn_pty::snapshot::render_html`. Bad format returns 400 with a short `unknown format '<x>'; expected one of text|ansi|html`; unknown agent returns 404 with `agent '<id>' not found`. Source bytes come from the tail of `{log_dir}/{id}/transcript.log` — `read_transcript_tail` seeks to `len - min(rows*cols*8, 256 KiB)` and reads forward, so the snapshot reflects recent output without pulling the whole transcript into memory. New `SessionManager::log_dir()` accessor exposes the manager's logging root to the endpoint. New integration test `crates/atn-server/tests/screenshot_endpoint.rs` boots the real binary on an ephemeral port, spawns a `fake-claude` agent, polls until the banner lands in a `text` screenshot, then exercises every response path (text default, explicit `text`, `html`, raw `ansi`, `format=bogus` → 400, unknown id → 404). Drive-by router fix: `process_outbox_file`'s DeliverToAgent branch previously sent the prompt text then slept 150ms before sending `\r`, which produced a pre-existing flake in `three_agent_topology_end_to_end` where `/api/events` could report only 1 of 2 delivered events because the second `event_log.push` hadn't run yet when the test's both-inboxes poll fired. Folded the text + `\r` into one `HumanText` so the event log is consistent with the inbox-write milestone. 5-run soak of the three-agent test now passes deterministically. cargo test + clippy + doc warning-free.

- Ops-polish saga step 2 (vt100-driven terminal snapshot core): new `atn_pty::snapshot` module that parses any PTY-output byte stream through a `vt100::Parser` at a given rows × cols geometry and produces a `TerminalSnapshot { rows, cols, cells: Vec<CellRow> }` with one `Cell { ch, fg, bg, bold, italic, underline, inverse }` per grid position. Three rendering targets share one source of truth: `render_text()` strips style, `render_ansi()` emits SGR escapes with per-row resets so paste-into-terminal behaves, and `render_html()` emits a self-contained `<pre>` with inline-styled `<span>` runs (coalesces same-style cells, maps the standard 16-colour + 6×6×6 cube + 24-step greyscale palette to CSS `rgb` hexes). Geometry is clamped to ≥ 1×1 to dodge vt100's zero-size panic. `vt100 = "0.15"` hoisted into the workspace dependency table so `atn-pty` and `atn-replay` both pull from the same pin. 11 unit tests cover cursor-move + overwrite, clear-screen, ANSI colour / bold round-trips, HTML escaping, zero-geometry clamp, empty-input grid, palette spot-checks, and the `same_style` cell coalescer. `atn-replay` left functionally unchanged (runs through its own `vt100` usage) — step 3 will cut the HTTP endpoint over to the new core; factoring `atn-replay` onto it is a follow-up if output ever diverges. cargo test + clippy + doc warning-free.

- Ops-polish saga step 1 (shell-escape for coordinator commands): fixes the `(priority: High)` bash-syntax-error known-issue in `docs/status.md`. New `atn_core::shell::shell_escape` helper wraps a string in single quotes and escapes interior single quotes with the classic `'\''` dance — the result is always a single POSIX shell word even when the input has parens, spaces, `<`, `>`, `$`, `|`, `&`, `;`, `"`, `'`, `\`, backticks, newlines, or unicode. `canned_action_to_bytes` in `atn-pty::writer` now pipes `CannedAction::ReadWiki.page` and `CannedAction::Ack.request_id` through the helper before formatting the `coord read …\n` / `coord ack …\n` lines that hit the PTY. `CoordinatorCommand { command }` and `setup_commands` are left unescaped by design (they're full shell snippets); `HumanText` stays as-is (LLM agents interpret text, not shell). 10 table-driven unit tests in `atn-core` cover the escape helper (empty string, interior quotes, parens+colon, metacharacter passthrough, unicode, the always-single-word invariant). New `atn-pty` integration tests drive a real bash PTY through `CannedAction::ReadWiki { page: "Requests (priority: High)" }` and `CannedAction::Ack { request_id: "REQ-can't-touch-this" }` and assert the output carries no `syntax error` / `unexpected EOF` and does carry `coord: command not found` — i.e. bash parsed the line as a single word. Ops-polish saga begins; the known-issue bullet for this bug is removed from `docs/status.md`. cargo test (workspace), clippy, doc all clean.

- Windowed-UI saga step 6 (persistence, docs, demo): closes the saga. New `localStorage` key **`atn-window-ui-v1`** carries just the windowed-UI state — `layoutMode`, `sortMode`, `selectedId`, and per-agent `windows[id] = { state, pinned, pinnedRect }` (omitting default-state windows to keep the blob small). `saveWindowUiPrefs` / `loadWindowUiPrefs` handle it; `saveLayoutPrefs` now fans out to both keys and `windowSelect` + `setSortMode` trigger saves so selection + sort choice survive a hard refresh. New top-bar **sort selector** (`Name` default / `Recent` = smoothed-heat-desc) is wired into `buildLayoutItems` so Tiled worker order + Carousel ring follow the choice. New `demos/windowed-ui/setup.sh` (1 coord + 3 workers, local-fake-shim default, `ATN_DEMO_REAL=1` opt-in) stands up the Demo 9 topology and prints a five-step pointer at layouts / chrome / keyboard / sort / persistence. New `docs/windowed-ui.md` walkthrough covers TL;DR, three layouts, chrome + pin semantics, top bar, Option-C keyboard, sparkline row, `atn-window-ui-v1` schema. `docs/demos-scripts.md` gains **Demo 9 "Windowed UI"** in the index + a full section; Demos 6 and 8 get `(legacy model)` banners pointing at Demo 9. `docs/scale-ui.md` banner at the top notes it's the legacy walkthrough and links to `windowed-ui.md`. `docs/usage.md` Dashboard section rewritten around windowed UI; links `docs/windowed-ui.md` first in the See-also list. `docs/status.md` rolls forward: **Current State** now reads "Windowed-UI Saga Complete" and W1..W6 rows (tiled-foundation, window-chrome, stack-layout, carousel-layout, keyboard-option-C, persistence-docs-demo) join S1..S8. Live smoke: 4-agent demo, state changes (stack + coord-pinned-at-rect + sort=recent) round-trip cleanly through a hard refresh; pinned rect `{143, 40, 1113, 645}` restored exactly, UI reflects pinned class + sparkline amber outline + dock population. `cargo doc --workspace --no-deps` warning-free; cargo test + clippy clean.

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(server): GET /api/agents/{id}/screenshot + router flake fix

Step 3 of the ops-polish saga. New endpoint returns a rendered
terminal snapshot for any live agent.

- `GET /api/agents/{id}/screenshot?format=text|ansi|html&rows=N&cols=N`
  (defaults 40x120, geometry clamped to 1..=500). `text` and `ansi`
  respond `text/plain`; `html` wraps the inline-styled `<pre>` from
  `atn_pty::snapshot::render_html`.
- `read_transcript_tail` seeks to `len - min(rows*cols*8, 256 KiB)`
  and reads forward — bounded memory, recent output only.
- 400 on bad format with a short human message; 404 on unknown id.
- New `SessionManager::log_dir()` accessor.
- Integration test `screenshot_endpoint.rs` boots the real binary
  on an ephemeral port, spawns a fake-claude, and exercises every
  response path (text default, explicit format, html, ansi, 400, 404).

Drive-by fix to `router::process_outbox_file`: replaced the
"send text → sleep 150ms → send \r" pair with a single HumanText
carrying `text + "\r"`. The sleep made `three_agent_topology_end_to_end`
flake because the test's both-inboxes poll could fire between the
second inbox write and the second `event_log.push`. 5-run soak
now deterministic.

cargo test + clippy + doc clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
